issue_title: Remove spaces in `extras_require`
issue_body:
Right now the [`extras_require`](https://github.com/loli/medpy/blob/9318c8edf61948870d47b55159984b6dc22edac3/setup.py#L134-L136) is:
```python
          extras_require = {
            'Additional image formats' : ["itk >= 3.16.0"]
          },
```
which contains a space in the key, something I have never seen before. This causes problems when trying to pip install. Perhaps there is a way to escape, but this seems non intuitive:
```bash
$ pip install "medpy[Additional image formats]"
...
pip._vendor.packaging.requirements.InvalidRequirement: Invalid requirement, parse error at "'[Additio'"
```

I suggest offering a string without a space.

issue_title: add UI support for watchpoints
issue_body:
* add UI support for breakpoints that have the type `watchpoint`

use the gdb mi [`-break-watch` command](https://sourceware.org/gdb/onlinedocs/gdb/Set-Watchpoints.html) to add a UI element where appropriate (i.e. on expression or memory) to set watchpoint on memory/expression 

* when *read from* (`-break-watch -r <expr>`)
* when *written to* (`-break-watch <expr>`)
* when *read from or written to* (`-break-watch -a <expr>`)

issue_title: Enhancement: Web push
issue_body: I am looking for a tool to let me send web push notifications to browsers. Even IE is adding support, and it'd be a killer way to reach users. Is that in scope?

issue_title: Prevent regenerating the model with each run
issue_body: We need a way to save the generated model so it can be updated instead of just regenerated, since the regeneration is very slow. This is the main issue that needs to be solved before `bbopt` is released publicly.

issue_title: ipdb autocompleter should be able to use "readlinelike" completions
issue_body:
It would be nice to have a configuration option for ipdb to have the autocompleter use the "old style readlinelike" completions.

Please have a look at http://ipython.readthedocs.io/en/stable/config/options/terminal.html for how IPython solves this:
```
TerminalInteractiveShell.display_completions : ‘column’|’multicolumn’|’readlinelike’
    Default: 'multicolumn'
```

issue_title: v0.8.1 doesn't persist state when stopped
issue_body:
Change introduced in e25e26f2cb628b025330a70ef2e2d0b888259d08 causes database state not to be saved.

**Configuration:**
```python
# flowerconfig.py
address = "127.0.0.1"
port = int("9000")
db = "/opt/flower/var/flower.db"
persistent = True
xheaders = True
debug = True
```

**What I did:**
```
$ pip install flower==0.8.0
...
$ /opt/flower/bin/celery flower --conf=/opt/flower/flowerconfig.py --broker='amqp://localhost:5672/myvhost' --broker_api='http://localhost:15672/api/'
...
<PRESS Ctrl+C>
[D 150424 15:53:45 events:96] Saving state to '/opt/flower/var/flower.db'...
```
Note debug message. I've also verified with a ipdb breakpoint.

```
$ pip install flower==0.8.1
...
$ /opt/flower/bin/celery flower --conf=/opt/flower/flowerconfig.py --broker='amqp://localhost:5672/myvhost' --broker_api='http://localhost:15672/api/'
...
<PRESS Ctrl+C>
```
No debug message is shown, and detected with a ipdb breakpoint that ``self.started``([here](https://github.com/mher/flower/blob/master/flower/app.py#L54)) is ``False``. This happens both with and without any events received.

issue_title: 406 Not acceptable on OS X
issue_body:
I am not sure why exactly but every simple request on OS X from `urlopen` and `urlretrieve` results in 406 errors.

OS X 10.10.4
Python 2.7.6

After some fiddling I realised, that it's related to missing the `User-Agent` header bit. I quickly fixed it by overwriting the global opener instance with one using a sensible `User-Agent` value.

The commit can be found here: https://github.com/trevex/glad/commit/e5815d1b950b0c242e052624c94b6b5c4498b2bb

I'll test it later on my desktop pc running Ubuntu to check whether it breaks anything. I am not an experienced python developer, so let me know if I should send a pull request or you'll fix this in a more idiomatic or cleaner way...

issue_title: UDP Server
issue_body:
I looked for this type of library when I found Twisted doesn't have python3.5 support. I watched a video on YouTube of you at a conference (which was humorous and very informative) and began to research to develop some concurrency for my specific purposes.  In the hunt, I came across curio and after reading a little from the Docs,  and long before reaching the end of the page, I knew that it was your code and decided to try to implement it.  
   That being said,I am instituting a UDP server with clients and was surprised that socket.DATA_GRAM isn't supported with curio."socket wrapper" ; curio.network.tcp_server is being called.  I looked through the Docs and did not see any mention of UDP or DATA_GRAM.  Will udp_server be supported with curio.socket?

issue_title: exp and log should return dimensionless quantity
issue_body:
`exp` and `log` rightly fail with `DimensionalityError` on any quantity that has a dimension.

But when it`s passed a dimensionless quantity, it does not return a dimensionless quantity; it returns a standard numpy array (or scalar):

```
In [85]: type(numpy.exp(ureg.Quantity(1, ureg.dimensionless)))
Out[85]: numpy.float64
```

This is inconsistent with the behaviour for other ufuncs, that always return pint quantities when passed a pint quantity.  For consistency, `log`, `exp`, and perhaps some others that I've missed,

issue_title: add a method to directly get the active namenode
In fish fuck needs $history[2] not $history[1]
issue_body: $history[1] holds the latest command, in this case `fuck`.

issue_title: Change "Interesting extension files"color
Automatic descent to find `sconscript` files should skip directories containing a nested `sconstruct` file
issue_body: Currently all directories are checked recursively from the same directory of the currently invoked `sconstruct` file. If there is a sub-project with its own `sconstruct` file that directory should be skipped as any nested `sconscript` files will actually belong to nested `sconstruct` file.

issue_title: Error running BESST 2.2.7
issue_body:
Hi,
We've tried to run BESST 2.2.7, but after the first step (pass1 of 4) it crashed. 
The results for the pass1 are already srtated to be written in the corresponding folder, but after that an error occurred. See below the last lines of the log file and the error prompted.  

The complete log and error file are attached, as well as the Statistics reported by BESST:
[BESST_scaffolding.error.txt](https://github.com/ksahlin/BESST/files/1417692/BESST_scaffolding.error.txt)
[BESST_scaffolding.log](https://github.com/ksahlin/BESST/files/1417688/BESST_scaffolding.log)
[Statistics.txt](https://github.com/ksahlin/BESST/files/1417689/Statistics.txt)

issue_title: Use generators more
issue_body: Really, there's no real reason to explicitly cast to `list` every time an iterable is pushed on the stack. Keeping iterables in generator form on the stack will reduce memory usage and increase performance. Lists should only be used when needed (like when printing).

issue_title: default_permissions opton?
issue_body:
Would it be possible to have yas3fs accept the fuse option "default_permissions"?

I have found that while I can easily mount a S3 bucket with yas3fs (many thanks, Danilo!), the mounted filesystem can be accessed and updated by anyone when it's mounted with the "allow_other" option. I want the filesystem security to be the same as for a local filesystem.

The fuse mount option "default_permissions" seems to achieve the results I want.
I made a local copy of the yas3fs __init__.py and added this mount option, then mounted a S3 bucket with the modified copy.
With this mount, a user can only see and update files according to the standard Unix permissions.

The only change I made to __init.py__ was to add "'default_permissions':True" to the array "mount_options".
A proper fix would be to allow this to be toggled off and on as a yas3fs argument, default being off so existing usage is not affected.

If there's a better way to ensure Unix permissions on a S3 filesystem are honored, I'd appreciate the advice.

Regards
Mal

issue_title: IOS command "remote command" not working
issue_body:
Hi,

I am experiencing intermittent failures when collecting data from a Cisco stack switch (WS-C3750G-48TS-S) device running IOS version 12.2(40)SE.
Sometimes netmiko returns full results and sometimes it returns a partial result. Sometimes it returns the end of the last command, when a new command is entered.
In this particular instance, the network connection is slow, however the CLI is returning full results.
I have tried using send_command() and send_command_timing() but the result is the same.

netmiko (1.4.3)
paramiko (2.3.1)

issue_title: Finding index of ngram
issue_body:
Im trying to put together a simple recipe for identifying common ngrams across multiple docs, reporting them using an nltk style concordance output which requires knowing the start and end index of a string that gets mapped onto a particular ngram.

Is there a way of getting this index information back for an ngram?

eg something that might return something like:

    b=TextBlob("The quick, brown fox and the quick, brown dog.")
    b.ngrams(2,offsets=True)

    [ (WordList(['The', 'quick']), [(0,8)]),
     (WordList(['quick', 'brown']), [(4,15), (29,40)]),
     (WordList(['brown', 'fox']), [(11,19)]), ...]

That way it would be easy enough to display the part of the original text that led to each ngram.

issue_title: 4scanner hashing all image even with ` "check_duplicate": false `
issue_body:
The hash file is created even when ` "check_duplicate" ` is set to false in the json config file.
This is a problem because the point of this option is to not create a hash file because it may be too slow (for example if you have 1 million picture in your download folder, or if you are downloading to slow nfs drive etc...)

To fix the issue, this function in download.py must only be called if ` "check_duplicate" ` is true:
` dupecheck.hash_img_in_folder(out_dir, img_hash_log) `

issue_title: non unicode data not correctly handled in Python 3
issue_body: Filenames and command line arguments can include (non-unicode) binary data on most operating systems.  This is more difficult to handle on Python 3 because these are generally expressed as the str type.  I've created pull request https://github.com/giampaolo/psutil/pull/733 with a test case that highlights the issue but do not have a solution at this point.

issue_title: Consumer offset migration from 0.9.5 KafkaConsumer to 1.x KafkaConsumer
issue_body:
When using `KafkaConsumer` from kafka-python 0.9.5, the consumer offsets are stored in ZooKeeper.
In kafka-python 1.x the offsets are stored in Kafka and it does not pick the offsets up from ZooKeeper, but starts at the end of the topic. This may cause messages to be skipped if the offsets are not migrated. I have tested this with broker version 0.9. 

According to Kafka [FAQ](https://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/KAFKA/FAQ#FAQ-HowdowemigratetocommittingoffsetstoKafka%28ratherthanZookeeper%29in0.8.2?) and [documentation](http://kafka.apache.org/documentation.html#offsetmigration) the migration is handled by setting `dual.commit.enabled=true` in the Java consumer temporarily to force the offset being copied from ZooKeeper to Kafka, then reconfiguring the consumers again to only commit to Kafka. As far as I can tell, kafka-python does not have such option.

What seems to work for us is a simple script that takes the offsets from ZooKeeper and commits them manually to Kafka using the new client, but this needs to be done with all consumers in the consumer group to be down at the same time.

Other options include adding dual commit support to python-kafka or creating new topics for new consumers (the latter requiring to reconfigure producers as well at the time of migration).

I miss a migration guide in the documentation so I'd like to contribute one. What are the recommended steps to upgrade consumers to use the new `KafkaConsumer` implementation?

issue_title: Files added in the first commit of a git repository don't appear to be searched
issue_body:
To run trufflehog on a set of files that aren't in a git repository, I decided to just add them to a new repository to make things work. In the process I noticed that files added in the very first commit don't seem to be searched / reported on by trufflehog.

## Reproduction steps

Here I create a new git repo in an empty directory, copy my SSH public key in to it, add and commit the file. Despite the high-entropy nature of my public key, trufflehog's output is blank:

```
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest▶ mkdir one 
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest▶ cd one
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/one▶ git init
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/tom/tmp/truffletest/one/.git/
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/one (master)▶ cp ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub ./
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/one (master)▶ git add id_rsa.pub 
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/one (master)▶ git ci -m "Adds public key"
[master (root-commit) b6ea149] Adds public key
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)
 create mode 100644 id_rsa.pub
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/one (master)▶ trufflehog .
```

In contrast, if I first add and commit an empty file to a fresh repository, and add my public key in a second commit, trufflehog finds and reports on it with no issue:

```
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest▶ mkdir two 
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest▶ cd two
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/two▶ git init
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/tom/tmp/truffletest/two/.git/
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/two (master)▶ touch empty
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/two (master)▶ git add empty
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/two (master)▶ git ci -m "Adds empty"
[master (root-commit) 9aa062a] Adds empty
 1 file changed, 0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-)
 create mode 100644 empty
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/two (master)▶ cp ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub ./
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/two (master)▶ git add id_rsa.pub 
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/two (master)▶ git ci -m "Adds public key"
[master 700191d] Adds public key
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)
 create mode 100644 id_rsa.pub
tom@bash:~/tmp/truffletest/two (master)▶ trufflehog .
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Reason: High Entropy
Date: 2018-02-08 14:00:52
Hash: 9aa062ab0c22e0d2788f2b0a1d03b635d71cfabc
Filepath: id_rsa.pub
Branch: master
Commit: Adds public key

@@ -1 +0,0 @@
-ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQ...[omitted to keep the screen width down :)]

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

issue_title: Tune default WT cache size
issue_body:
Granted that most uses for `mlaunch` are for reproduction cases and attach to `localhost`, can I recommend that the default value for `--wiredTigerCacheSizeGB` be set to a very conservative value.  I would suggest either the minimum (256MB) or another low default (1GB).

Alternatively, when initialising replsets/clusters, divide the default cache size (50% of RAM or some other lower value) by the number of nodes to prevent RAM oversubscription by the cumulative WT caches.  For example:

- WT cache = RAM * 0.5 / nodes * 2

or 

- WT cache = 256MB

issue_title: Log parser ignores lines with newlines in queries
issue_body:
mloginfo, mlogvis, and mplotqueries don't seem to handle logs containing queries containing strings with newlines in them.  For example, the WRITE log in this example reports on an update operation that inserts an XML document containing multiple newlines.  It is followed by a COMMAND log reporting a find operation with a simple query against a different collection:

```
2016-06-14T23:01:00.886Z I WRITE    [conn78263] update mydb.clxn1 query: { _id: { $eq: "id1" } } update: { $setOnInsert: { text: "<?xml version="1.0"?>
<xxxxxx type="Axxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx" xmlns="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx" xmlns:ac="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx">
  <xxxxxxxx ..." } } keysExamined:1 docsExamined:1 nMatched:1 nModified:0 keyUpdates:0 writeConflicts:0 numYields:1 locks:{ Global: { acquireCount: { r: 2, w: 2 } }, Database: { acquireCount: { w: 2 } }, Collection: { acquireCount: { w: 2 } } } 129ms
2016-06-14T23:01:05.886Z I COMMAND  [conn78357] command mydb.clxn2 command: find { find: "clxn2", filter: { _id: "id2" }, limit: 1, singleBatch: true } planSummary: IDHACK keysExamined:1 docsExamined:1 idhack:1 cursorExhausted:1 keyUpdates:0 writeConflicts:0 numYields:1 nreturned:1 reslen:387 locks:{ Global: { acquireCount: { r: 4 } }, Database: { acquireCount: { r: 2 } }, Collection: { acquireCount: { r: 2 } } } protocol:op_query 113ms
```

If I save this in a file and run mplotqueries against it, mplotqueries only plots the second query, after writing this:

```
SCATTER plot
   id   #points  group
    1         1  mydb.clxn2
```

If I run mloginfo again the file, it reports both timestamps but reports the number of lines, not the number of records:

```
     source: mongodb.log.broken
       host: unknown
      start: 2016 Jun 14 23:01:00.886
        end: 2016 Jun 14 23:01:05.886
date format: iso8601-utc
     length: 4
     binary: unknown
    version: = 2.6.x (iso8601 format)
    storage: unknown
```

It looks like the issue is with the _iterate_lines method in mtools/util.logfile.py, which could benefit from a wrapper around the line iterator that just joins lines with no leading timestamp to the preceding line.

issue_title: Two memory leaks in authenticate_gss_server_step
issue_body:
For reference, I'm looking at the code as of commit fbfc8254b4775a05a6d046cea04fdbdcf66c4ce9.

In `authenticate_gss_server_step`, there are two small leaks.  Both occur when `state->server_creds == GSS_C_NO_CREDENTIAL` (line 766).

First, `target_name` is used with `gss_inquire_context` and then with `gss_display_name` on line 776, but the storage allocated for it from the `gss_inquire_context` is never freed with `gss_release_name`.

Second, storage is allocated for `output_token` via the call to `gss_display_name` on line 754.  That is fine, but when `state->server_creds == GSS_C_NO_CREDENTIAL`, `output_token` is reused at line 776 in another call to `gss_display_name` without first having been freed with `gss_release_buffer`.

issue_title: Feature Request: Allow compare files helper function to be used publicly
issue_body:
In using XlsxWriter to create Excel files, we would like to write unit tests to confirm that the XlsxWriter generated file matches our expectations. Using diff or filecmp returns False, even when both the testing and expected files are generated using XlsxWriter. XlsxWriter currently has a helper function _compare_xlsx_files to achieve this in its own tests. It would be helpful to those using this module to be able to import that function for testing within our own applications.

Currently, I'm using a workaround of a wrapper around the internal function in a [forked repo](https://github.com/Dharma-Platform/XlsxWriter/commit/26c19a2594c6b15afc4051e4fc234010a60af957).

issue_title: Is sqlalchemy an implementation detail?
issue_body:
Currently, the only way I can see to catch things like foreign key constraint is to catch the `sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError` raised and handle it. However, this makes me uncomfortable since there is no indication if sqlalchemy is currently just an implementation detail or a core part of `records`.

Can I safely handle exceptions raised by sqlalchemy and call it a day or should I be worried about stuff breaking if sqlalchemy gets swapped out for something else? If no, is there an official way of catching errors raised by foreign key constraints?

issue_title: Race condition in Xboard test?
issue_body:
`test_human_offer_draw_during_engine_search()` in test.py:
```
    def test_human_offer_draw_during_engine_search(self):
        draw_handler = chess.xboard.DrawHandler()
        self.engine.draw_handler = draw_handler

        self.mock.expect("nopost")
        self.engine.nopost()
        self.mock.expect("st 10")
        self.engine.st(10)
        self.mock.expect("go")
        self.engine.go(async_callback=True)

        self.mock.expect("draw", ("offer draw", ))
        self.mock.expect("?")
        self.engine.draw()

        time.sleep(0.01)

        self.assertEqual(self.engine.end_result, chess.xboard.DRAW)
        self.mock.assert_done()
```
If i run `./test.py -vv XboardEngineTestCase` a lot, occasionally it fails at chess/xboard.py:1068 which is an Exception due to the engine being terminated earlier than it was supposed to be. I'm not sure why this is happening. This doesn't seem to occur for the other tests so I can only assume it happens due to async_callback=True but I'm not sure...

issue_title: Macro plugins will not be executed when macro start tag contains parameters
Helper get_id method for resources
flake8 broken by pyrestcli's future==0.15.2 dependency
issue_body:
The version of `future` that `pyrestcli` currently uses (0.15.2) causes flake8 errors for us, although the connection is a little complicated. 

We added `carto-python` (https://github.com/CartoDB/carto-python/) to our project which depends on `pyrestcli`, and `pyrestcli` depends on `future==0.15.2`. This was the first package in our project to include `future`. 

When our continuous integration server tested the change, we suddenly got a lot of flake8 errors in files that should be excluded from flake8 coverage. We found out that this is a known problem when flake8 is run in an environment with `future` installed —
https://gitlab.com/pycqa/flake8/issues/221

The problem is related to future’s implementation of `configparser` which was removed in 0.16.0 —
https://github.com/PythonCharmers/python-future/issues/118

TL;DR - `pyrestcli` uses a version of `future` that breaks flake8; changing `pyrestcli` to use `future` >= 0.16.0 should solve the problem. 

I understand that you might have other reasons that you don't want to upgrade the `future` version.  Thanks in advance for your consideration.

issue_title: Ignore whitespace
issue_body:
Hello.  I am requesting **a feature to ignore whitespace**, which I can use in my calculator expressions, but can be applicable in general.  

*Problem*: I have a grammar that parses the expression `2+3`, but it does not parse `2 + 3`, raising `ParseError: Rule 'operator' didn't match at ' + 3'`.

```python
# Starting grammar
from parsimonious import grammar

grammar = grammar.Grammar(
    """
    expression = left binary_op right
    left       = number
    right      = number
    number     = ~"\d+"i
    
    binary_op  = plus
    plus       = "+"
   
    """
)
```

*Approach 1*: I can fix this issue by modifying the operator with regex i.e. `plus = ~"\s*\+\s*"`.   I get the following result:

```python
# AST (clean)
>>> tree = grammar.parse("2 + 3")
>>> print(tree)
<Node called "expression" matching "2 + 3">
    <RegexNode called "number" matching "2">
    <Node called "operator" matching " + ">
        <RegexNode called "plus" matching " + ">
    <RegexNode called "number" matching "3">
```

However, if I extend this grammar, I would prefer not to modify every new operator with surrounding `\s*` tags.  

*Approach 2*: Alternatively, I could assign an optional item that sees zero or more occurrences of a `whitespace`:

```python
"""
    ...

    binary_op   = whitespace* binary_op_ whitespace*
    binary_op_ = multiply / divide / plus / minus 
    plus       = "+"
    minus      = "-"
    multiply   = "*"
    divide     = "/"
    whitespace = ~"\s"

"""
```
While this works, I have an extra dummy variable `binary_op_`.   The list of such variables increases for other optional characters, such as parentheses.  Furthermore, the AST gets littered with extra "spacing" nodes, which in my case, makes an more nested tree than is required.

```python
# AST (less clean)
>>> tree = grammar.parse("2 + 3")
>>> print(tree)
<Node called "expression" matching "2 + 3">
    <RegexNode called "number" matching "2">
    <Node called "binary_op" matching " + ">
        <Node matching " ">                                                 # spacing node
            <RegexNode called "whitespace" matching " ">
        <Node called "binary_op_" matching "+">
            <Node called "plus" matching "+">
        <Node matching " ">                                                 # spacing node
            <RegexNode called "whitespace" matching " ">
    <RegexNode called "number" matching "3">
```

1. **Is there an idiom for ignoring  whitespace** in parsimonious?  
1. If not, I think a **cleaner alternative may be to have a method that ignores a given regex**, [similar to `rply`][1] (e.g `grammar.ignore("\s")` for whitespace). This feature should allow `grammar.parse("2 + 3")` to return the same "clean" AST as `grammar.parse("2+3")`.

  [1]: https://rply.readthedocs.io/en/latest/users-guide/lexers.html

Hi,
I just wanted to check if there is a way to force xmlsec.SignatureContext.sign to include the `<X509SubjectName>` and `<X509IssuerSerial>` tags while signing xml documents.

Right now, I'm doing this (most of the code is taken from the `test_sign_generated_template_pem_with_x509` method in `test_sign.py`)

``` python
    root_node       = etree.fromstring(xmlstr)

    signature_node  = xmlsec.template.create(root_node,
                                             xmlsec.Transform.EXCL_C14N,
                                             xmlsec.Transform.RSA_SHA1)

    root_node.append(signature_node)

    # Add the <ds:Reference/> node to the signature template.
    ref = xmlsec.template.add_reference(signature_node, xmlsec.Transform.SHA1)

    # Add the enveloped transform descriptor.
    xmlsec.template.add_transform(ref, xmlsec.Transform.ENVELOPED)

    # Add the <ds:KeyInfo/> and <ds:KeyName/> nodes.
    key_info = xmlsec.template.ensure_key_info(signature_node)

    # Load private key (assuming that there is no password).

    key = xmlsec.Key.from_file(filename, xmlsec.KeyFormat.PEM, '<password>')

    # Load the certificate and add it to the key.

    key.load_cert_from_file(filename, xmlsec.KeyFormat.PEM)

    # Set key name to the file name (note: this is just a test).

    # Set the key on the context.

issue_title: LAYOUT must allow empty match
issue_body:
* parglare version: bc33add9eedbea64e5177fd716865cdb8360aa15 (Jan 27, 2018)
* Python version: 2.5.3
* Operating System: linux, ubuntu

### Description
The following specification breaks on missing whitespace at the start of the text:
``` python
from parglare import Grammar
from parglare import Parser

gram = """\
words : word | words word ;
word : /[a-z]+/ ;

LAYOUT : WS | comment ;
comment : /#.*/ ;
WS : /[ \t]+/ ;
"""

text = "abc def"

grammar = Grammar.from_string(gram)
parser = Parser(grammar)
result = parser.parse(text)
print(result)
```
produces
``` python traceback
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "n.py", line 17, in <module>
    result = parser.parse(text)
  File "~/compiler3/parglare/parser.py", line 208, in parse
    position)
  File "~/compiler3/parglare/parser.py", line 475, in _skipws
    input_str, position, context=context)
  File "~/compiler3/parglare/parser.py", line 282, in parse
    nomatch_error(actions.keys()))
parglare.exceptions.ParseError: Error at position 1,0 => "*abc def". Expected: WS or comment
```
If you take out the `LAYOUT` and `comment` rules, it works. It looks like there is a forced `LAYOUT` at the start of the file. There may also be one at the end of the file, but that is not testable currently, I think.

I know forcing space between tokens is not normal in programming languages, as people tend to be afraid of using the spacebar and write code like `a+1-4=b`. However, the language I am parsing is a constrained natural language with sentences like `power-supply must provide power`. Optional white space doesn't make much sense there, and (I think) complicates parsing due to non-existing white-space ambiguities that must be resolved (words should never be broken into two pieces).

Depending on how you see this problem, several options to fix it exists (for as far as I can see):
* Note in the discussion of `LAYOUT` that it must allow to be empty.
* Don't require a `LAYOUT` at the start of the file, ie make it optional (the text might start with white-space, but not always).

Likely other options exist as well.

issue_title: Allowing only members of a group to see a forum
issue_body:
Hi, I have a question about how to do something with the permission system:
I want all forums to be accessible to everyone, except for one, which should be accessible only to the members of a specific group.

The way you do this in most forum software _(and what I tried to do)_ is:
- _Globally allow everyone access to all forums (I did this with a "Users" group, which everyone is in)_
- Add a negative (`Permission.has_perm =  False`) access permission for the forum to everyone
- Add a positive (`Permission.has_perm =  True`) access permission to the specific group

Because the positive comes after the negative, most forum software will give the members the permission. In machina however, this makes the forum inaccessible to everyone.

This is caused by the fact that the default permission checker doesn't respect the order of permissions. Specifically, [this line](https://github.com/ellmetha/django-machina/blob/master/machina/apps/forum_permission/checker.py#L90-L92), which filters out all permissions that have a corresponding negative one. While this is more predictable, it is also a lot less flexible.

My question is the following: 
Is this a bug and a PR fixing it would be accepted, or is this an intentional feature and I'm missing some very obvious way of achieving this?

issue_title: unicodecsv is kind of slow; but maybe unavoidable?
issue_body:
Thank you so much for unicodecsv, it's been a big help for me in Python2. Not to sound ungrateful, but...

unicodecsv seems fairly slow. Some benchmarking suggests it's about 5-6x slower than the plain Py2 csv module. Of course it's doing more work, decoding bytes to strings! But for comparison the Py3 csv module (which does decoding) is only 2-3x slower than Py2. Is there room for improvement in unicodecsv?

I did some profiling and code reading and didn't see any obvious way unicodecsv could be made faster. So maybe there's no real way to optimize it. But wanted to file the issue both to document what I learned and get a second opinion.

My benchmark code and results are at https://nelsonslog.wordpress.com/2015/02/26/python-csv-benchmarks/

issue_title: linebreaks(self.content) breaks EntryPlaceholder HTML
issue_body:
Since EntryPlaceholder content may contain anything, for instance Javascript code, the linebreaks() call will often break the output. Also, it is not necessary since the CMS plugins take care of their own formatting.

Suggesting this working fix (override html_content()):



	"""Placeholder model for Zinnia"""
	from cms.models.fields import PlaceholderField
	
	from zinnia.models import EntryAbstractClass
	
	
	class EntryPlaceholder(EntryAbstractClass):
		"""Entry with a Placeholder to edit content"""
	
		content_placeholder = PlaceholderField('content')
	
		class Meta:
			"""EntryPlaceholder's Meta"""
			abstract = True
	
		@property
		def html_content(self):
			"""No additional formatting is necessary"""
			return self.content

issue_title: Bad error handling regarding event listener signature
issue_body:
Let's consider the following setup: we use `socketio` with some `asyncio`-based server (like `sanic`). We subscribed to some custom message and expect just one data argument for it:
```python
from sanic import Sanic
import socketio

app = Sanic(__name__)
sio = socketio.AsyncServer(async_mode='sanic')
sio.attach(app)

@sio.on('custom message')
async def on_custom_message(sid, data):
    print('Got data {} from sid {}'.format(data, sid))

app.run()
```
Now we connect to this server and send message with incorrect data values count:
```js
var io = require('socket.io-client');
var socket = io();
socket.emit('custom message', 1, 2);
```
Here is what we get in the server's logs:
```
2017-06-20 00:03:30 - (sanic)[INFO]: Goin' Fast @ http://127.0.0.1:8000
2017-06-20 00:03:30 - (sanic)[INFO]: Starting worker [29888]
2017-06-20 00:03:32 - (network)[INFO][127.0.0.1:34022]: GET http://localhost:8000/socket.io/?EIO=3&transport=polling&t=Lp2Akw4&b64=1  200 117
yo
2017-06-20 00:03:32 - (sanic)[ERROR]: Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/sanic/app.py", line 471, in handle_request
    response = await response
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/engineio/asyncio_server.py", line 135, in handle_request
    socket = self._get_socket(sid)
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/engineio/server.py", line 411, in _get_socket
    raise KeyError('Session is disconnected')
KeyError: 'Session is disconnected'

2017-06-20 00:03:32 - (network)[INFO][127.0.0.1:34028]: GET http://localhost:8000/socket.io/?EIO=3&transport=polling&t=Lp2Akwd&b64=1&sid=68f480e234f546f980340d107e3cda62  500 144
2017-06-20 00:03:32 - (sanic)[ERROR]: Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/sanic/app.py", line 471, in handle_request
    response = await response
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/engineio/asyncio_server.py", line 137, in handle_request
    packets = await socket.handle_get_request(environ)
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/engineio/asyncio_socket.py", line 73, in handle_get_request
    return await getattr(self, '_upgrade_' + transport)(environ)
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/engineio/asyncio_socket.py", line 120, in _upgrade_websocket
    return await ws(environ)
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/engineio/async_sanic.py", line 122, in __call__
    await self.handler(self)
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/engineio/asyncio_socket.py", line 138, in _websocket_handler
    await self.send(packet.Packet(packet.NOOP))
  File "/tmp/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/engineio/asyncio_socket.py", line 53, in send
    raise IOError('Socket is closed')
OSError: Socket is closed

2017-06-20 00:03:33 - (network)[INFO][127.0.0.1:34030]: GET http://localhost:8000/socket.io/?EIO=3&transport=polling&t=Lp2Al5F&b64=1  200 117
2017-06-20 00:03:33 - (network)[INFO][127.0.0.1:34034]: GET http://localhost:8000/socket.io/?EIO=3&transport=polling&t=Lp2Al5Q&b64=1&sid=b13efb25fbd945f580bf7e5333623304  200 3
```

As a result, original exception (`on_custom_message() takes 2 positional arguments but 3 were given`) is not displayed at all, the only thing suggesting what happened is a mysterious `yo` message which is followed by some internal errors.

I think we could at least print basic exception information before disconnecting socket (see `asyncio_server.py` line `164`), because disconnection itself (together with its event handlers) may produce some other errors which will not allow us to see the original error.

issue_body:
I wanted to create a user with predefined password for a test so i did this:
````python
    import autofixture

issue_title: Write unit-test that shows incorrect handling of overridden id field.
issue_body:
Suppose that I've overridden `id` field on a Document with an arbitrary datatype (non-ObjectId) and created a default serializer for it:

```
class MyDocument(Document):
    id = StringField(primary_key=True, required=True)

class MySerializer(DocumentSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = MyDocument
```

Currently, DRF-M handles this situation incorrectly. If I pass data={id: "SomeUniqueKey"} to MySerializer, it successfully passes `is_valid()`, but later on fails to save the document.

I have to do the following:

1. Write a unit-test that indicates to this incorrect behavior.
2. Fix this behavior.
3. Look into handling of field arguments more attentively, may be more bugs are hiding there.

issue_title: Fix typos and replace tearDown() with doCleanups().
issue_body:
- [x] Rename all test_retrival to test_retrieval - typo error
- [x] Replace all `tearDown()` occurrences with `doCleanups()` in unit-tests. The difference is that `tearDown()` is called only upon exit of a unit-test with success of failure status, while `doCleanups()` works even with errors in unit-test or in `setUp()`. As we need our mongo databases cleaned up irrespective of test outcome, I think, we'd better use `doCleaunps()`.

issue_title: PReLU in gluon
issue_body:
class PReLU(gluon.HybridBlock):
        def __init__(self, *args):
            super(PReLU, self).__init__(*args)
            with self.name_scope():
                self.alpha = self.params.get('alpha', shape=(1,), init=mx.init.Zero())
                self.act = gluon.nn.Activation(activation='relu')

        def hybrid_forward(self, F, x, *args, **kwargs):
            pos = self.act(x)
            neg = F.negative(self.alpha.data()) * self.act(F.negative(x))
            return pos + neg

This custom block only works for non-hybridized models (error below), but would be nice to see this as a default activation option, something similar to this: "gluon.nn.Activation(activation='prelu')" - Is this in the plans going forward?

    AssertionError: Argument data must be Symbol instances, but got 
    [ 0.]
    <NDArray 1 @cpu(0)>

issue_title: Why is num_epoch the parameter of FeedForward initializer instead of parameter of fit method in python model api
issue_body:
I think it's will be more straightforward if pass num_epoch to `fit` method instead of pass it to `FeedForward` class initializer, because num_epoch shouldn't be regarded as an attribution of model. It's a training argument. 

The current implementation is inconvenient if I want to train a model on a training data  for several epochs and then train the model on another train data for epochs. 

There is same issue on `initializer` and `optimizer` parameter

issue_title: Installing django-wkhtmltopdf fails on fresh requirement install
issue_body:
A lot of travis like environments (like GitLab CI) always install all requirements for testing purposes and often use twelve-factor configuration with environment variables. (http://12factor.net/)

This code in will fail on install because Django is not installed yet:
https://github.com/incuna/django-wkhtmltopdf/blob/master/wkhtmltopdf/__init__.py#L2-L3

A better way must be provided to ensure the installation of the product. 
A quick-and-dirty way is to use a try/catch ImportError.

```
try:
    if 'DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE' in os.environ:
        from .utils import *
except ImportError:
    pass
```

issue_title: Decorator wrapping when creating new Resource class
issue_body:
Every time new Resource class instantiates, methods (get, post, patch, put) are wrapped with check_headers decorator. Wrapping occures on class level. For example, after creating ten Resource classes, there are ten same decorators wrapping get method.
https://github.com/miLibris/flask-rest-jsonapi/blob/master/flask_rest_jsonapi/resource.py#L39
Since Flask [creates new instance](https://github.com/pallets/flask/blob/master/flask/views.py#L83) of View class (which is inherited by Resource) with every incoming request, in the end there are lots of same decorators wrapping (get, post, patch, put) methods. That causes RecursionError at some point.

issue_title: Exponential backoff timeout?
issue_body:
I am using boto3/botocore in AWS Lambda to perform some management tasks against the AWS API. I was noticing that some of my lambda functions would timeout when I started hammering the API and was getting throttled. It appeared that Boto3 was using it's exponential backoff logic as intended. What I am trying to figure out is if there is any kind of timeout logic to the exponential backoff logic. It appears that there is a maxRetries option available, but other than that is Boto3/botocore's default behavior to keep retrying the API with exponential backoff until AWS returns a 503/RequestLimitExceeded error or succeeds?

I am trying to figure out a good balance for my Lambda timeout time between letting Boto retry and not having my lambda run for 5 minutes.

issue_title: Gene Set Enrichment: output and input
issue_body:
Gene set enrichment widget should output the data profiled with selected genes. The data format should follow the format of input data: if the genes are in rows, so should be in the output, if in columns, the same. The output should also pass the information on genes and organism.

Currently, upon selection of gene sets in this widget, the widget does not produce any output.

I was testing the widget with brown-selected data set (File -> Gene Name Matcher -> Gene Set Enrichment). Note that the selection of, say, biosynthetic process, should output the data containing 139 genes (in rows) and all the data (all columns). Note that selection of several gene sets should produce the data with union of genes from the sets.

Test also this functionality on single cell data, where the number of columns (genes) on the output should be reduced, while the number of rows (cells) should be retained.

Note: keep meta and class data, if in input, on the output as well.

Minor:
- rename "Gene" input channel to "Data" (to conform to what all other widgets use)
- "Missing Gene ID information..." -> "Missing gene ID information..." (g is in lowercase)
- When arbitrary Orange file is on input, the widget throws correct error (missing gene ID info...), but still displays "No data on input." in the info box. Change this message to "Input data with incorrect meta data.\n Use Gene Name Matcher widget."

issue_title: Run in Pipfile
issue_body:
It would be nice to declare run in Pipfile like:

```
...
run= main.py
...
```
and run it like: `pipenv run`
instead of `pipenv run main.py`

issue_title: Silent no error message / no Pipfile.lock when resolver can't find stable package combination
issue_body:
Be sure to check the existing issues (both open and closed!).

When resolver can't find a stable set of dependencies, `pipenv install` (and probably `pipenv lock`) doesn't generate a lockfile but also doesn't generate any visible error (nor does it install anything).

Please run `$ python -m pipenv.help`, and paste the results here.

If you're on MacOS, just run the following:

    $ python -m pipenv.help | pbcopy

##### Expected result

Expected either dependencies to be resolved and packages installed or a visible error message to be printed to the console.

##### Actual result

```
›› pipenv install
Pipfile.lock not found, creating…
Locking [dev-packages] dependencies…
Locking [packages] dependencies…
```

Nearly full output of [pipenv install --verbose here](https://gist.github.com/jtratner/bd73a7a0f344551ce3deaa47875a11ed)

I unfortunately fixed the issue by wiping my caches away. With `--verbose` (before wiping away cache) I saw a bunch of messages about unstable rounds that look like:

```
New dependencies found in this round:
  adding ['pytest', '==3.4.2', '[]']
Removed dependencies in this round:

  removing ['py', '>=1.5.0', '[]']

------------------------------------------------------------
Result of round 9: not stable
```

```

  adding ['attrs', '>=17.2.0', '[]']

------------------------------------------------------------

```

```

  adding ['pytest', '==3.4.2', '[]']
Removed dependencies in this round:
  removing ['attrs', '>=17.2.0', '[]']
  removing ['colorama', '', '[]']

```

and the final output was just "Result of round 16: not stable" - with no additional message to the user.

##### Steps to replicate

as I said, can't really replicate it because it stopped failing after I wiped my wheel cache, but here's the Pipfile:

```

issue_title: Invalid Pipfile.lock causes crash with JSONDecodeError
issue_body:
##### Issue description

If the Pipfile.lock is invalid json, e.g. like [this example](https://gist.github.com/konstin/fa95a8630257ea90c1f1ed28a6615d75) which has a merge conflict, pipenv crashes with a JSONDecodeError

##### Expected result

Pipenv would either print an error that the Pipfile.lock is invalid or override the invalid file (I'd prefer the latter).

##### Actual result

```
$ pipenv install

    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/konsti/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 697, in main
    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
  File "/home/konsti/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 1066, in invoke
    return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx))
  File "/home/konsti/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 895, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)

    parse_constant=parse_constant, object_pairs_hook=object_pairs_hook, **kw)

issue_title: Handling of unusual version strings (eg. containing 'dev')
issue_body:
Install process fails when the version string is not in the typical x.y.z format. To be exact the installation succeeds but the Pipfile and Pipfile.lock are not getting updated.
$ python -m pipenv.help output Pipenv version: `'2018.05.18'` Pipenv location: `'/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv'` Python location: `'/usr/bin/python'` Other Python installations in `PATH`: - `2.7`: `/usr/bin/python2.7` - `2.7`: `/usr/bin/python2.7` - `2.7`: `/bin/python2.7` - `3.6`: `/usr/bin/python3.6m` - `3.6`: `/usr/bin/python3.6` - `3.6`: `/bin/python3.6` - `3.6.5`: `/usr/bin/python` - `3.6.5`: `/bin/python` - `2.7.15`: `/usr/bin/python2` - `2.7.15`: `/bin/python2` - `3.6.5`: `/usr/bin/python3` - `3.6.5`: `/bin/python3` PEP 508 Information: ``` {'implementation_name': 'cpython', 'implementation_version': '3.6.5', 'os_name': 'posix', 'platform_machine': 'x86_64', 'platform_python_implementation': 'CPython', 'platform_release': '4.14.32-1-lts', 'platform_system': 'Linux', 'platform_version': '#1 SMP Sun Apr 1 11:41:45 CEST 2018', 'python_full_version': '3.6.5', 'python_version': '3.6', 'sys_platform': 'linux'} ``` System environment variables: - `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` - `LANG` - `GDM_LANG` - `DISPLAY` - `EDITOR` - `COLORTERM` - `MOZ_PLUGIN_PATH` - `_CFLAGS` - `XDG_VTNR` - `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` - `SOUND_FILE` - `RAX_SSO` - `XDG_SESSION_ID` - `XDG_GREETER_DATA_DIR` - `USER` - `DESKTOP_SESSION` - `PWD` - `HOME` - `LESSHISTFILE` - `SSH_AGENT_PID` - `_LDFLAGS` - `LIBVIRT_DEFAULT_URI` - `TMUX` - `XDG_SESSION_TYPE` - `HISTFILE` - `XDG_SESSION_DESKTOP` - `VIRSH_DEFAULT_CONNECT_URI` - issue_title: auditwheel should replace the linux tag with a manylinux1 tag instead of adding a new tag
issue_body:
`auditwheel` has a command named `addtag` to rename a wheel file to insert the `manylinux1_x86_64` platform tag into its filename (in addition to the existing `linux_x86_64`) and similarly for `manylinux1_i686` / `linux_i686`.

This is an issue because uploading wheel files with the `linux_x86_64` / `linux_i686` platform tags to warehouse or PyPI is forbidden (while  `manylinux1_x86_64`/ `manylinux1_i686` are authorized or will soon be).

Instead `auditwheel repair` should probably replace the `linux` based tags by `manylinux1` tags whenever the `repair` operation succeeds.

issue_title: Polling order
issue_body:
The poller demonstrates "preferences" for some projects over others
and lets them monopolize process slots.

The following code in `poller.py`
polls projects in whatever order `iteritems()` uses
which is arbitrary but constant.
```python
for p, q in self.queues.iteritems():
    c = yield maybeDeferred(q.count)
    if c:
        msg = yield maybeDeferred(q.pop)
        returnValue(self.dq.put(self._message(msg, p)))
```
This becomes obvious when multiple projects
have several spiders queued and waiting for slots.
The first `(project, queue)` pair returned from `iteritems()`
that has queued spiders
will prevent any further projects from being polled.
If its queue is constantly full
only this project will have its spiders scheduled.

Possible solutions:

One way to solve this is suggested by @luc4smoreira in #140.
However it can't be a correct solution (for this issue)
because it can make total slot utilization vary a lot.
E.g. with 5 slots per project and 10 projects,
when 1 project is active there will be only 5 slots utilized
(even if the single active project is in need for more)
but when all 10 are active, there will be 50 slots.
In a workaround for the above, one can set the total slot limit
just above the slots per project
multiplied by the number of projects expected to monopolize slots
so that there will always be a few slots for others.

Another incomplete solution is to use the spider priority earlier in the poller
before picking a project. When I noticed this problem, I first tried lowering the priority
of spiders belonging to the project monopolizing the slots
and was surprised because it had no effect.

We can also stop using multiple queues
and keep everything in a single database.
Although it sounds like a big change,
by doing it we'll drop a lot of code from scrapyd
and we can even implement both of the 2 suggestions above
cleaner, faster and with less code.

issue_title: Test-mode bank account creation when testing throws wrong error message
issue_body:
Here is the procedure I followed when testing adding an external account to a Connect `Account` that was already created:

```
>>> my_account = stripe.Account.retrieve(...)
>>> my_account.external_accounts.create(external_account={
    'object': 'bank_account',
    'account_number': 'DE89370400440532013000',
    'country': 'FR',
    'currency': 'eur'
})
InvalidRequestError: Request ...: You cannot use a live bank account number when making transfers or debits in test mode
```

The `account_number` value comes from https://stripe.com/docs/connect/testing#creating-accounts

The solution was actually to change the `country` value to `DE` because the IBAN account number seemingly was German-style.

issue_title: Cannot Use .save() to Change Plans
issue_body:
Please clarify in the API docs that updating an active subscription using `sub.save()` will not upgrade or downgrade the subscription plan.

Example code:
```python
subscription = stripe.Subscription.retrieve('sub_49ty4767H20z6a')
subscription['items']['data'][0].plan.id = new_plan
subscription.save()
```

An API call must be initiated via `stripe.Subscription.modify()`. Using `save()` will only result in a call to `util.logger.debug("Trying to save already saved object %r", self)` and otherwise does nothing.

issue_title: file_delete of CHGCAR/WAVECAR and nscf runs
issue_body:
## System

Most recent custodian run on NERSC

## Summary

* Many VASP custodian handlers have a _file_delete action of CHGCAR and WAVECAR as part of their operation as part of the VASP "recommendation"
* However, non-SCF runs (where the CHGCAR is held fixed) requires these files to be present. If they are deleted and VASP is restarted, the job will immediately fail.

## Error message

```
 reading WAVECAR
 WARNING: chargedensity file is incomplete
 ERROR: charge density could not be read from file CHGCAR for ICHARG>10
 ERROR: charge density could not be read from file CHGCAR for ICHARG>10

 ERROR: charge density could not be read from file CHGCAR for ICHARG>10
 ERROR: charge density could not be read from file CHGCAR for ICHARG>10
 ERROR: charge density could not be read from file CHGCAR for ICHARG>10
```

## Suggested solution (if known)

Handlers that delete the CHGCAR/WAVECAR should first check whether ICHARG > 10. If it is, then do not perform the deletion of CHGCAR/WAVECAR.

issue_title: TM: Synapse Creation on every permanence adjustment
issue_body:
Not sure about what is going on with Python but I noticed something that could have tremendous memory pressure and performance impact if I'm right about what I'm seeing...?


In connections.py https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/nupic/research/connections.py#L194


``` Python
def updateSynapsePermanence(self, synapse, permanence):
    """
    Updates the permanence for a synapse.

    @param synapse    (int)   Synapse index
    @param permanence (float) New permanence
    """
    self._validatePermanence(permanence)

    data = self._synapses[synapse]
    newData = SynapseData(data.segment,   <--------------------   HERE
                          data.presynapticCell,
                          permanence)
    self._synapses[synapse] = newData

    # Update indexes
    self._synapsesForPresynapticCell[newData.presynapticCell][synapse] = newData
```

Every time the permanence is adjusted on a Synapse, a new SynapseData object is created?

(the line starting with: "newData = SynapseData(...)" )

When running a simple test in a short lived method such as extensive_temporal_memory_test.py I count something upwards of around 700 thousand Synapses! ...and 60 thousand Segments!

This line is called from "adaptSegment()" (line ~617) - EVERY TIME ADAPT SEGMENT IS CALLED!

I'm not sure how good the garbage collection is in Python (or even if it has garbage collection), but  this data should be indexed in a map and not created upon every adjustment in my opinion?

This needs to be a mutation instead of creation...?

issue_title: WebSession: support for multiple emails
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-03-21

Up to know the email address played a central role in Invenio, often considered as unique identifier for a user.

With Invenio supporting multiple authentication method (possibly concurrently), it is very likely that user have multiple email addresses that he might desire to be recognized in Invenio.

This require amending the current database tables structure in particular by introducing a new table `userEMAIL`

e.g.:

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS userEMAIL (
  id_user int(15) unsigned NOT NULL,
  email varchar(255) NOT NULL default '',
  priority int unsigned NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY email (email),
  KEY id_user (id_user),
) ENGINE=MyISAM;
```

The `email` column in `user` table could be kept in order to maintain track of the *preferred* email address for the user for communications.

Special care must be devoted in adapting APIs (e.g. `collect_user_info` and friends in order to take into consideration the availability of more than one email address.)

issue_title: api: bulk indexing two or more times a record
issue_body:
### Problem
Sometimes happen that we add a record inside the bulk queue two o more times in a short period (because e.g. different celery tasks are working with it).
We were wondering if we can/need to optimize the [bulk operation](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-indexer/blob/master/invenio_indexer/api.py#L179) from DB side and from ES side. 

### Questions

1. Does `elasticsearch-py` handle automatically this situation?

2. Could we optimize in a way that a record is read from the database no more than one times?

3. Could we maybe use the `Record.get_records()` to improve the read instead of a lot of `Record.get_record()`? 

My [first attempt](https://github.com/hachreak/invenio-indexer/tree/56_bulk_index_improve) to not indexing a record two times.
For sure we can find a better solution, but it can give you an idea to what I mean.

------------------------------------------
cc @egabancho

issue_title: Boolean Index query result is incorrect when inverted+resultset
issue_body:
This might not be an issue, but a lack of understanding.  
I do know however that one of our queries is misbehaving:

```
query_request={ 'path': '/somewhere/below/something',
                               'boolIndex': True
                         }
```

The indexed value is "False", so it's an inverted query.

What is returned:
`difference(resultset, index)`

And I read that as:
"Subtract the indexed values from the result set (all objects satisfying the path query) and return it"
Which is not correct.

Shouldn't this be:
"Invert the index" `difference(self._unindex, index)`  And then intersect that with the result set `intersection( <that>, resultset)`

Also, we have an inverted index and have not modified it, so eventually _invert_index() will run and the error goes away - running a different line of code in _apply_index() (or query_index())  However, I'm not convinced this happens ONLY on inverted indexes.   Queries for the unindexed value could suffer the same error.

This code is more than 7 years old and I can't possibly be the one to discover this error, so I'm really hesitant to raise the "Bug Flag".

issue_title: Scheduler failover flag is unsettable
issue_body:
There is no way to set the `_failover` flag in the scheduler driver. The current behavior means that if the scheduler throws an unexpected exception, all the tasks running on that framework are immediately terminated.

There should be a supported way to set this value to `True`.

        device = adb_commands.AdbCommands.ConnectDevice(rsa_keys=[signer])
        # Now we can use Shell, Pull, Push, etc!
        apk_path = device.Shell('pm path ' + base)

        new_path = apk_path[apk_path.startswith("package:") and len("package:"):]

        # a = unicode(new_path, "utf-8")
        device.Pull(new_path)


    except CalledProcessError as e:
        print(e)`

If this isn't supported, could it be? I wouldn't mind working on a PR to add this functionality if it would be useful. It seems like perhaps simply supporting a `raw` kwarg for the `add` or `add_callback` method may be sufficient, with a way to support iterators.

issue_title: `sentry_version` clarification
issue_body:
According to the release log from 1.0.4 of raven-python, `sentry_version` is the expected sentry server version. [1]

However, in the unit tests and the latest official sentry Dockerfile as of today, that SENTRY_VERSION has been inconsistent specified: Dockerfile has it defined as '8.10' [2] unit tests in Sentry server has it fixated at '2' [3] and in `raven-python` fixated at '6'. [4]

So those brought up my question: does `sentry_version` actually matter in the header that raven client sent in the header? If not, can we ignore it from the header?

[1] https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/master/CHANGES#L579
[2] https://github.com/getsentry/docker-sentry/blob/master/8.10/Dockerfile#L56
[3] https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/master/tests/base/tests.py#L195
[4] https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/blob/master/tests/sentry/web/api/tests.py#L83

issue_title: Unable to drag with KendoUIs sortable widget
issue_body:
I'm switching from Font Awesome 4.5 to 5.0 (SVG with Javascript). It took me a while to find this: I have some DIVs in vertical placed order. All those have a little DIV in it with a drag symbol (fa-sort) to show the user he can sort all vertical placed DIVs. The drag functionality (Kendo UI widget) does not work if the user clicks the mouse button while the mouse pointer is placed on the fa-sort icon. It works only if the user hits the DIV area around the fa-sort icon (see attachment). I don't know how to fix this. It looks like the icon blocks to call the drag operation. I have implemented it that way: `
` (dragdiv class the one supporting drag operations) Previously I have used Font Awesome as CSS class and this was never an issue. Now, with "SVG with Javascript" I came up to this problem. This is no issue on clicking (buttons) on a font awesome icon, just dragging it. Maybe this is also a problem with other Javascript frameworks supporting drag operations. I just have found this with Kendo UI (Safari, Chrome, FF). issue_title: Error for proxy models.
issue_body:
When trying to generate reports that include a model that is marked as proxy=True, the api seems to fail. 

I found the fix (it all comes to using ContentType.objects.get_for_model with the parameter for_concrete_model set to False, so ContentType for proxy models are also retrieved)

Furthermore, there are other related bugs in model introspection util functions, as fields from proxy models are not retrieved using the meta method get_all_field_names() (get_fields() must be used instead). However this fix might break retro compatibility ... 

If you want I can make a pull request....

issue_title: [Proposal] Chalice invoke command
issue_body:
Overview
---------
With all of the new event types being released into Chalice, it would be great if Chalice had a built-in way to invoke the deployed Lambda function (and possibly a local version). Right now, I am invoking these functions through the console or the AWS CLI, but it would be much more convenient if Chalice had a built-in `invoke` command that accepts:

* `--name <function-name>`: The logical name of the Lambda function in the chalice app
* `--stage <stage-name>`: To specify the stage you want to target
* `--payload <value>`: A JSON payload to use when invoking the Lambda function

It would then print the result of the invoke to standard output.

Example
--------
Using this deployed `app.py`:
```python
from chalice import Chalice

app = Chalice(app_name='intro')


@app.lambda_function()
def hello(event, context):
    return {'hello': 'world'}


@app.lambda_function()
def hello_name(event, context):
    return {'hello': event['Name']}
```
Users would then be able to invoke the deployed Lambda functions manually:
```
$ chalice invoke --name hello
{"hello": "world"}
$ chalice invoke --name hello_name --payload '{"Name": "Kyle"}'
{"hello": "Kyle"}
```

Future additions
----------------

* Preformatted event payloads. Not sure how this would look quite yet, but essentially you can just provide the only the important event parameters to `chalice invoke` (i.e. bucket/key for S3 event or message for SNS event) and chalice will take the parameters and format an event on your behalf based on what the event source is and invoke the function.

* Local mode. Potentially add a `--local` flag so this will invoke your Lambda function in your Chalice application locally.

* `--context <context-value>`. Add the ability to send a context as well as a payload to invoke the Lambda function.

issue_title: `with ComputationGraph():` syntax?
issue_body:
From my own experience with new dynet (python) users, one of the most common mistakes is forgetting to renew the computation graph (in particular at test time where it sometimes doesn't run out of memory but becomes super slow).

What do you think of adding something like:

```python
with ComputationGraph():
    # do stuff within one computation graph
# Every expression here is stale
```

I don't think it would be more work and it could easily be made backward compatible by keeping `renew_cg` and just rewriting the docs to promote the new preffered syntax.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Too many lookup parameters in populated model
issue_body:
TLDR: Upon loading a model from a file that I have just built, the programme throws the following error:

> RuntimeError: Too many lookup parameters in populated model at /_21

Specifically, the follow code was adapted from https://github.com/jcyk/greedyCWS
(The original code works under Python 2 and Dynet 1.0, however, I have adapted part of the code to make it work under Python 3 and Dynet 2.0)

Character embeddings is first loaded, where it will then be used to initialise the CWS object.  This CWS object is a dynet model object with the specified structured defined under initParams.  Suppose I specify the saved model file under load_params, the CWS object will attempt to populate the model based on the previously saved files containing the model using CWS.save(filename) where self.model.save(filename) will be called subsequently.  This is where the runtime error comes in.  I have traced this error to be originated from dynet/io.cc, however, I have no clue why this error is prompted, or how I can adapt this code to make the programme work.

    Cemb, character_idx_map = initCemb(char_dims,train_file,pre_trained)
    cws = CWS(Cemb,character_idx_map,options)

    if load_params is not None:
        cws.load(load_params)
        test(cws, dev_file, 'result')


where CWS is defined as:

    class CWS (object):
        def __init__(self,Cemb,character_idx_map,options):
            model = dy.Model()
            self.trainer = dy.MomentumSGDTrainer(model,options['lr'],options['momentum'])
            self.params = self.initParams(model,Cemb,options)
            self.options = options
            self.model = model
            self.character_idx_map = character_idx_map
            self.known_words = None

        def load(self,filename):

            self.params['word_embed'] = self.model.add_lookup_parameters((len(known_words),self.options['word_dims']))

                params['com_b'].append(model.add_parameters(options['word_dims']))
            params['<BoS>'] = model.add_parameters(options['word_dims'])
            return params

```Python
>>> from gcloud import storage

>>> key = storage.key.Key(bucket = bucket, name = 'dir/file.webp', properties = {'contentType': 'image/webp'})

Success?

'image/webp'

'image/webp'

```

issue_title: Core library does not include init in root path
issue_body:
There is clearly a hierarchy in this package, for example "google.cloud.storage" is a particular package that includes the google.cloud core library. But there is no upstream "google" library, and so there is no associated `google/__init__.py` file created, which can lead to a painful debugging situation where simple code like "import google.cloud.storage" fails.

In depth:

This is a corner case -- but allowing for that: the wheel packages for Google Cloud Python are setup to use subfolders, which is relatively unique in Python packaging. So these packages are all underneath the "google/" folder. Unfortunately, there is no root package -- because the Google Cloud Core is not actually a root package, it too is a subfolder.

The consequence is that there is no `google/__init__.py` and there is no package that would own that file as a distributable wheel. This has some unfortunate side effects when attempting to do an import google.cloud.storage or similar when using Spark.

The workaround is simply to touch that file when packaging dependencies.

issue_title: spanner library doesn't work with credentials whose scope can't be modified.
issue_body:
I'm using application default credentials to create credentials for use with the spanner client library. These credentials are obtained by running 

```
gcloud auth application-default login
```

My code fails with the following stack trace.

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/bazel_cache/_bazel_jlewi/1c9ff9fd5966eae5ae66af2948992bce/bazel-sandbox/3357018790234970416/execroot/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/bazel-out/local-fastbuild/bin/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner_e2e_test.runfiles/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner_e2e_test.py", line 57, in testInsertSql
    (rowid, customer_number, run_id, event_log_id, path, offset))
  File "/bazel_cache/_bazel_jlewi/1c9ff9fd5966eae5ae66af2948992bce/bazel-sandbox/3357018790234970416/execroot/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/bazel-out/local-fastbuild/bin/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner_e2e_test.runfiles/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/tensorboard/db.py", line 390, in execute
    self._delegate.execute(sql, parameters)
  File "/bazel_cache/_bazel_jlewi/1c9ff9fd5966eae5ae66af2948992bce/bazel-sandbox/3357018790234970416/execroot/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/bazel-out/local-fastbuild/bin/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner_e2e_test.runfiles/org_tensorflow_tensorboard/tensorboard/platforms/gcp/spanner.py", line 221, in execute
    with self.database.batch() as batch:
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/spanner/database.py", line 401, in __enter__
    session = self._session = self._database._pool.get()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/spanner/pool.py", line 228, in get
    session.create()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/spanner/session.py", line 98, in create
    api = self._database.spanner_api
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/spanner/database.py", line 165, in spanner_api
    credentials = credentials.with_scopes((SPANNER_DATA_SCOPE,))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/oauth2/credentials.py", line 119, in with_scopes
    'OAuth 2.0 Credentials can not modify their scopes.')
NotImplementedError: OAuth 2.0 Credentials can not modify their scopes.
```

The problem appears to be the following code from [database.py](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/spanner/google/cloud/spanner/database.py#L164)

```
    @property
    def spanner_api(self):
        """Helper for session-related API calls."""
        if self._spanner_api is None:
            credentials = self._instance._client.credentials
            if isinstance(credentials, google.auth.credentials.Scoped):
                credentials = credentials.with_scopes((SPANNER_DATA_SCOPE,))
            self._spanner_api = SpannerClient(
                lib_name='gccl',
                lib_version=__version__,
                credentials=credentials,
            )
        return self._spanner_api
```
My credential is of type [google.oauth2.credentials.Credentials](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-auth-library-python/blob/master/google/oauth2/credentials.py#L118) which doesn't implement with_scopes.

The docs don't indicate a preferred pattern for distinguishing between credentials whose scope can be modified and those that can't. A simple solution would be to catch the NotImplementedError and ignore it.

issue_title: Address differences between pants.ini default interpreter constraints and target-level interpreter constraints
issue_body:
For Python interpreter selection in the v2 Pants backend, there can be differences between default interpreter constraints defined in a repo-owner controlled pants.ini and target-level constraints defined by other users. With the current selection logic, it is possible for the interpreter constraints in pants.ini to be completely ignored in favor of the user-defined, target-level interpreter constraints. 

The scope of this ticket is to devise a way to address this potential mismatch, and code up validation logic to enforce the best way to resolve this difference. 

This change would likely take place in the `setup` method of `PythonInterpreterCache` located in src/python/pants/backend/python/interpreter_cache.py. As it stands today, the `filters` variable takes precedence over `self._python_setup.interpreter_constraints`.

issue_title: JvmTarget.provides needs to accept more metadata to enable maven-central publishing
issue_body:
Right not provides accepts an [Artifact](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/artifact.py#L24-27) which is just an org, name, artifact repo url and description.  Sonatype requires more metadata in the pom we create so we need to provide a way for target authors to add this metadata.  The failure for `yes | ./pants clean-all publish src/java:: --no-publish-dryrun` looks like so:

![failed sonatype publish](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/157586/7109035/035ee6f6-e153-11e4-94b9-72bdc6558644.png)


At a minimum we need:

+ Project name
+ Project URL
+ SCM URL
+ Developer information
+ License

Right now we have a license, but its [baked in the pom template](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/templates/jar_publish/pom.mustache#L19-25).
We have an Artifact description but its not plumbed to the template.

See the full requirements here: http://central.sonatype.org/pages/requirements.html

issue_title: Robust `Client.close()`, no client/solver/future reuse after close
issue_body:
After `Client.close()`:
- all workers have to be killed, immediately if user doesn't want to wait for results (which they are now too, but in a blocking manner)
- all solvers have to be invalidated, so no future submissions are possible (channel is closed),
- all futures have to be resolved (with timeout/shutdown error if `Client.close(wait=False)`)

issue_title: Add maximization method to factors
FDKFeatureCompiler is broken on POSIX
issue_body:
This commit which added `shell=True` to the subprocess invocation of makeotf may have fixed it for windows, but broke it for POSIX:

https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/commit/6d10690d321b3c7f3120e8b7107406ee8449f5fa

Quoting [subprocess docs](https://docs.python.org/3/library/subprocess.html#subprocess.Popen)

> On POSIX with shell=True, the shell defaults to /bin/sh... If args is a sequence, the first item specifies the command string, and any additional items will be treated as additional arguments to the shell itself. That is to say, Popen does the equivalent of:

     Popen(['/bin/sh', '-c', args[0], args[1], ...])

So on Unix, fontmake is trying to call makeotf as: `/bin/sh -c makeotf -f font.ttf -ff feautures -o output.ttf`, which means that the shell is only treating the first argument after -c as the command, the rest is ignored as those are not shell options, but options to the makeotf command...

Interestingly nobody complained in the last 8 months...
Which makes me think not many people rely on this feature.

I would actually like to kill it, or at least remove it from fontmake proper, and maybe readd it later as a new optional ufo2ft feature compiler class.

But in any case, we should implement this properly, and with tests, otherwise it gets unnoticed like now.

Perhaps we can do that after makeotf will return a sensible exit code, instead of always success, leaving us in the position of having to parse its output to determine whether it has failed or not:
https://github.com/adobe-type-tools/afdko/issues/417

Or even better, if ever MakeOTF.py exposed some function that we could import and call directly from fontmake instead of having to create temporary files and spawn a new process.

issue_title: "data", "response" and "status_code" executor methods as attributes
issue_body:
This is a design decision. I propose changing ``data``, ``response`` and ``status_code`` executor methods to be accessed as attributes instead of methods.

Current behaviour:
``` python
>>> response.some_data().data()
"this is a raw data"
```

Proposed behaviour:
``` python
>>> response.some_data().data
"this is a raw data"
````

issue_title: Release 0.7.0
issue_body:
Below are preliminary plans for things to do in release 0.7.0.

- [ ] remove support for old-style courses (Coursera shut down old courses anyway)
- [ ] remove `--ignore-http-errors` and make its behavior default
- [ ] implement quiz downloading and conversion to HTML

In release plans I usually prefer to put something that resembles features and visible changes. I don't put refactoring and bugfixes (except my huge ones).

@rbrito Another thing. I'm still thinking about https://github.com/coursera-dl/edx-dl/issues/77. I've registered mooc-dl package on PyPI. I hope we can put common code from coursera-dl and edx-dl there and quietly, under-the-hood, transition to it.

Let's not make loud statements but rather take small steps towards implementing it. I'm not sure how much time I can devote to it (new amazing courses on Coursera and EDX are coming up again and again) but at least let's make this first step. @rbrito Could you please create `mooc-dl` repository in this organization and grant me read-write access? Generally, I think youtube-dl and you-get projects should be inspiring for us and we should learn from them.

issue_title: Valid certificate rejected with TLS: Unknown CA
issue_body:
Hey ya'll,

I'm having some trouble that appears to be TLS related.

Here is the minimum reproduction:

`
from autobahn.twisted.wamp import Session
from autobahn.twisted.wamp import ApplicationRunner

from twisted.internet import reactor

def onClose(wasClean, code=None, reason=None):
    print(reason)


def create_disconnect_monkeypatch():
    """https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-python/issues/559"""
    def connect_success(proto):
        orig_on_close = proto.onClose

        def fake_on_close(*args, **kwargs):
            if proto._session is None:
                onClose(*args, **kwargs)
            else:
                orig_on_close(*args, **kwargs)

        proto.onClose = fake_on_close
    return connect_success


class Test(Session):

  def onJoin(self, *args, **kwargs):
    print("Joined!")

application_runner = ApplicationRunner(
  url="wss://hyperdash.io/api/v1/sdk",
  realm="hyperdash.sdk",
)

application_runner_deferred = application_runner.run(
  Test,
  start_reactor=False,
  auto_reconnect=False,
)

application_runner_deferred.addCallback(
    create_disconnect_monkeypatch(),
)

reactor.run()
`

issue_title: Capital Letters in Registered URIs
issue_body:
Capital letters are not allowed in registered URIs. For example, `@register('example.camelCaseFails')` fails:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3.6
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from autobahn.wamp import register
from autobahn.twisted.wamp import ApplicationRunner, ApplicationSession

class Example(ApplicationSession):
    @register('example.snake_case_succeeds')
    def foo(self):
        pass

    @register('example.camelCaseFails')
    def blah(self):
        pass

ApplicationRunner(None, None).run(WampServices)
```

Raises

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./autobahn-register-camel-case.py", line 7, in <module>
    class Example(ApplicationSession):
  File "./autobahn-register-camel-case.py", line 12, in Example
    @register('warden.camelCaseFails')
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/autobahn/wamp/uri.py", line 343, in decorate
    f._wampuris.append(Pattern(real_uri, Pattern.URI_TARGET_ENDPOINT, options))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/autobahn/wamp/uri.py", line 215, in __init__
    raise Exception("invalid URI")
Exception: invalid URI
```

Googling, the only related discussion I found was

https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/autobahnws/mkjF21Fb8ow

Why, by default, are capital letters (e.g. for camelCase) not allowed in registered URIs?
