issue_title: TiffStack fails on IndexError from Tifffile
issue_body:
Reported to me via email:

    >>> v=pims.TiffStack(f)
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
      File "/home/christopher/.virtualenvs/wormvision3/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pims/tiff_stack.py", line 322, in __init__
        self.im.seek(j)
      File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/PIL/TiffImagePlugin.py", line 626, in seek
        self._seek(frame)
      File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/PIL/TiffImagePlugin.py", line 644, in _seek
        self.tag.load(self.fp)
      File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/PIL/TiffImagePlugin.py", line 417, in load
        for i in range(i16(fp.read(2))):
      File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/PIL/_binary.py", line 35, in i16be
        return (i8(c[o])<<8) | i8(c[o+1])
    IndexError: index out of range

I usually pass a file name, not file object, to `TiffStack` but file objects work fine too:

    In [2]: f = open('stuck.tif', 'r')

    In [3]: pims.TiffStack(f)
    Out[3]: 
    <Frames>
    Source: <open file 'stuck.tif', mode 'r' at 0x105d95810>
    Length: 5 frames
    Frame Shape: 512 x 512
    Pixel Datatype: <type 'numpy.uint16'>

It looks like this issue goes down all the way to tifffile. I'm not sure we can do anything except maybe fail more informatively.

[//]: # (
. Note: for support questions, please use Stackoverflow or Gitter**. 
. This repository's issues are reserved for feature requests and bug reports.
.
. In case of any problems with Allure Jenkins plugin** please use the following repository 
. to create an issue: https://github.com/jenkinsci/allure-plugin/issues
.
. Make sure you have a clear name for your issue. The name should start with a capital 
. letter and no dot is required in the end of the sentence. An example of good issue names:
.
. - The report is broken in IE11
. - Add an ability to disable default plugins
. - Support emoji in test descriptions
)
Hi !

#### I'm submitting a ... 
  - [x] bug report
  - [ ] feature request
  - [ ] support request => Please do not submit support request here, see note at the top of this template.

#### What is the current behavior?

Test names are not properly utf8-encoded in results files.

#### If the current behavior is a bug, please provide the steps to reproduce and if possible a minimal demo of the problem

Here is a very simple test. 

```python
# test_allure.py

def test_musée(self):
    assert False
```

JSON output:
```json
{"name": "test_musÃ©e", "status": "failed", "statusDetails": {"message": "AssertionError: assert False", "trace": "E   assert False"}, "start": 1527000563172, "stop": 1527000563173, "uuid": "80c6f937-6366-4a1c-a194-f159517e1e4a", "historyId": "b9238153ea1ae00f41b1b2da19fba81e", "fullName": "test_allure#test_musÃ©e", "labels": [{"name": "host", "value": "adrien-XPS"}, {"name": "thread", "value": "9176-MainThread"}, {"name": "framework", "value": "pytest"}, {"name": "language", "value": "cpython3"}, {"name": "package", "value": "test_allure"}]}
```

#### What is the expected behavior?

UTF8 names are preserved.


#### Please tell us about your environment:

- Allure version:        2.6.0
- Test framework:        pytest@3.5.1
- Allure adaptor:        allure-pytest@2.3.3b1

#### Other information 
I suppose that [`escape_name`](https://github.com/allure-framework/allure-python/blob/master/allure-pytest/src/utils.py#L102) function is to blame here. But I don't really understand how it is used and why.

issue_title: Error processing response in Django middleware when another request middleware short-circuits
issue_body:
The Django insights middleware crashes in `process_response` when a middleware short-circuits when processing the request because the `process_request` hasn't be run.

For example, the `CommonMiddleware` will short-circuit to affix a missing trailing slash with a 301 Moved Permanently response if the client accesses `/admin` when `/admin/` is the registered URL

```
MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES = [
    # ...
   'django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware',
    # ...
   'applicationinsights.django.ApplicationInsightsMiddleware'
]
```

The reasonable solution is probably to check for the presence of the `appinsights` attribute in the `process_response` method. The method can submit the event without the `duration` field set, if the `appinsights` attribute is missing.

issue_title: Support LUN Snapshot Creation and Deletion
is windows 32bit support complex to add ? (for python-3.5+)
Re-evaluate how peer HEAD chain info is handled.
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

When we connect to a peer we extract some information from the `Status` message to store as indicators to the peer's current chain head.

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/trinity/protocol/eth/peer.py#L78-L79

Then, whenever a `NewBlock` message comes through, we update this information

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/master/trinity/protocol/eth/peer.py#L49-L53

This update **assumes** multiple things about the info transmitted via `NewBlock` which I do not believe we can reliably trust.

- That the block is genuine
- That the block's parent is canonical

It appears that under these assumptions a malicious peer should be capable of luring trinity towards a header hash that is entirely bogus.

### How can it be fixed

On connection, request the most recent `N` headers leading up to the peer's `head_hash`.  This will be referred to as the "recent headers" chain.

When a `NewBlock` arrives validate it against the chain of `N` headers we have *on-file*.  Using the block number from the new block, request the ancestor chain for the new block far enough back to reliably link up with the known recent headers.  This should probably include a few extra headers to account for small chain re-orgs if the new block happens to be on a longer fork chain from the known recent header chain.  If the new block can be validated against the peer's recent headers then extend the recent header chain up to the new block and discard the oldest headers to keep the recent headers list under a max size `N`

Validating the recent header chain should be as thorough as possible without incurring unreasonable overhead.

The recent header chain can also likely be a shared resource across multiple peers to avoid re-requesting the same headers from each peer.

issue_title: Autoconfigured web3
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

In code examples, and short scripts, it's a real PITA to show the ramp-up needed to get web3 working. It conveys that things are going to be tedious (an unfair first impression, I hope):

```
from web3 import Web3, IPCProvider

w3 = Web3(IPCProvider())
```

### How can it be fixed?

Instead, I would love to have an autoconfigured web3, especially for some default cases, like localhost on 8545, and the geth IPC file.

```
from web3.auto import w3
```

This could try to connect to IPC in common locations and then localhost:8545

Or if you are running both IPC and HTTP, and want to give it a hint, that might look like:

```
from web3.auto.http import w3
```

issue_title: new type of package dependence: dependence on executables only
issue_body:
When package  A depends on package B, but only needs from package B some executable files/scripts (and not libraries or include files), then package B can be installed in a separate conda environment.   Then any conflicts between B and its dependencies, and any other packages needed by A, can be completely avoided.  The separate conda environment would be put under the main conda environment (say under etc/env_B ).   For each executable in package B, say etc/env_B/bin/myprog , we create an executable script in the main conda environment (  bin/myprog ) which temporarily activates B's conda environment etc/env_B , then runs the original executable in etc/env_B/bin/myprog, then deactivates B's conda environment.

Thus, for example, if the package A needs python 3, it can still depend on a package that needs python 2, as long as it only needs executables from that package and not libraries.

To implement this, a new type of 'requirements' would be added to meta.xml; say, 'requirements-executables' .

issue_title: JSON output for errors should be properly formatted instead of text
issue_body:
Here's example output:

```json
{
  "error": "No packages found in current osx-64 channels matching: ipython-notebookzzz\n\nDid you mean one of these?\n\n    ipython-notebook\n\nYou can search for this package on Binstar with\n\n    binstar search -t conda ipython-notebookzzz\n\nYou may need to install the Binstar command line client with\n\n    conda install binstar",
  "error_type": null
}
```

Note all of the new lines.  There's valid structured data hidden in there, but it requires that I write a text processor in each environment I want to use it in.  Here's an alternative:

```json
{
  "error": "No packages found in current osx-64 channels matching: ipython-notebookzzz",
  "packageName": "ipython-notebookzzz",
  "channels": [
    "http://example.com/foo/",
    "http://example.com/bar/"
  ],
  "platform": "osx-64",
  "alternatives": [
    "ipython-notebook"
  ]
}
```

Note, this problem is created by the `conda.cli` modules providing formatted text data rather than raw python data structures to an output/render function that then handles formatting the display depending on the context (see [these lines][1] for an example).  To make these changes we'll either need to refactor `common.error_and_exit` or write a text parser inside it.

[1]: https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/41607615d422a3b20c9a485bf070c3cdd7c29de2/conda/cli/install.py#L355-L371

issue_title: no_proxy environment variable support
issue_body: I'm experiencing issues while trying to use the default conda channels from behind a proxy, together with a custom channel, located on the intranet. From what I'm seeing, conda correctly recognizes the http_proxy/https_proxy environment variables, but it is not affected by the no_proxy environment variable settings. Apparently conda suffers from an issue very similar to what I found described here: https://bitbucket.org/hpk42/devpi/issue/88/no_proxy-environment-variable-support

issue_title: Unintentional build downgrades in mixed feature settings
issue_body:
Under certain limited circumstances involving features, `conda` unexpectedly downgrades to an earlier build of a package. The conditions are as follows:

- The current version of a package `foo` has multiple builds with distinct build numbers.
- Newer builds depend on a second package `bar`, while older builds do not.
- Some of the valid choices for the `bar` dependency have features, and some do not.

The bug can be demonstrated in conda 4.3 with the command
```
CONDA_SUBDIR=XXX conda create -n errtest -c YYY python
```
where `XXX` and `YYY` are redacted pending confirmation we can share the info publicly (ask me or @msarahan for details, if needed). In this case, the latest build of python should be installed, but a much earlier build is installed instead.

Currently, this is not occurring with conda 4.4. The reasons for this are not fully clear, because the bug has been located and exists in 4.3 and 4.4. Bug fix coming.

issue_title: Support for environment activation/deactivation on Cygwin
issue_body:
On Cygwin, a `source activate` followed by a `source deactivate` breaks bash and zsh, the PATH is lost, even `ls` is a command not found. Upon issuing the deactivation command, a CondaValueError is reported 

> CondaValueError: Could not find environment: /cygdrived:\Miniconda3

A possible solution from Richard Pelvin can be found on this [thread](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45095500/conda-activate-deactivate-environment-breaking-path/47043344#47043344). I think the bugs should be easy to fix based on his work (See snippets [here](https://bitbucket.org/snippets/plevin/xon8rb/activatecygwin-anaconda-activate-script) and [there](https://bitbucket.org/snippets/plevin/BprGro/deactivatecygwin-anaconda-deactivate))

By the way, I don't know where in the ~~hell~~ code base one can apply those fixes. Where are these two files located?

issue_title: Cannot be installed with Numpy 1.10 due to incorrect package comparison operator
issue_body:
Hi, installing `bcolz` with Anaconda 2.4.0 fails. Anaconda 2.4.0 ships Numpy 1.10.1. Numerically, 1.10 is less than 1.7, but version-wise it is greater. Unfortunately `bcolz` uses an invalid comparison operator: 

https://github.com/Blosc/bcolz/blob/2851981f220b8a4dbc8c63ddede8e1f6dac0380c/pavement.py#L47
In short:

```
>>> '1.9.2' > '1.7'
True
>>> '1.10' > '1.7'
False
>>> '1.10.1' > '1.7'
False
```

issue_title: Data loss when fetching from S3/HTTP/HTTPS
issue_body:
Using pysam 0.11.2.2 linked against libcurl (version tbd), I was fetching 4,000 BCF files via signed S3 HTTPS URLs one chromosome at a time by specifying both BCF and index URLs.  A very few number of those fetches failed, but either due to a failure in htslib or in pysam, the failure was not retried until success nor was the error propagated to the calling code.  This failure led to silent data loss.

I'm going to investigate more deeply and request clarification from the htslib developers on how error and retry handling are performed when htslib is linked against libcurl.

issue_title: Filter on arbitrary flags in pileup (feature request)
issue_body: The `stepper` argument in the `pileup` method allows iterating through all reads (`="nofilter"`), or using two different predefined filters (`="all"` or `="samtools"`). The "all" option ignores reads with the any of the BAM_FUNMAP, BAM_FSECONDARY, BAM_FQCFAIL, BAM_FDUP flags. Would it be possible to allow the `pileup` method to filter based on any combination of any flags? For example, I would like to only ignore duplicate reads (BAM_FDUP, 0x400) and secondary alignments (BAM_FSECONDARY, 0x100).

issue_title: CheckboxSelectMultiple styles poorly
issue_body:
If I have a m2m field and switch the widget to using CheckboxSelectMultiple, the styling looks very poor--it uses the same bulleted-list style as the default Django admin UI, but looks a bit worse as the CSS makes the checkbox vertically higher than expected. This is the same whether I use the django-bootstrap3 rendered or not.

(Note that if I make a form manually using django-bootstrap3 with a CheckboxSelectMultiple field, it appears in a more bootstrap-appearing form)

issue_title: Filter snapshots based on duration
issue_body: It would be nice to be able to filter snapshots based on duration, so that crashed snapshots (e.g. `state = IN_PROGRESS && duration > 24h`) could be cancelled automatically.

issue_title: Support wait_for_completion on forcemerge action?
issue_body:
I'm running curator on AWS Lambda and it works great. I'd like to run `forcemerge` periodically but this operation can take a really long time to finish on large indices. Setting an absurdly high value won't work because AWS-managed Elasticsearch has a hard timeout on how long requests can run, and even if this were possible, Lambda hard-limits requests to 5 minute durations.

Would it make sense to support `wait_for_completion` on `forcemerge` to improve this situation? Thanks!

issue_title: Add support for client certificates
issue_body:
It would be great if Curator allowed specifying a client certificate for PKI/x509 support when communicating to a protected Elasticsearch cluster. 

The ES Python client already supports this, I believe: 

http://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.org/en/master/transports.html#elasticsearch.connection.Urllib3HttpConnection

issue_title: Reset connections when forking
issue_body:
I have an indexing process that is partly CPU-bound on the server that grabs the data and feeds it to ElasticSearch. I'm trying to parallelize the CPU-bound parts of that process with `multiprocessing`.

Unfortunately, the first action in the subprocess fails with a timeout because this library maintains a connection pool and sockets don't survive forking. I would like to close the connections just before forking. Is there a way to do that?

My alternative is to recreate the connection, but I have a layer of abstraction (`djangoes`) that gets in the way.

EDIT: in fact it doesn't get in the way, I could fix my problem with:

    from djangoes import connections
    del connections['xxx']

I'm OK with closing this issue as wontfix, but some documentation around connection persistence would be nice.

issue_title: Allow setting assert_hostname when using urllib3
issue_body:
In some instances, it may be necessary to set the `assert_hostname` when using the built-in `urllib3.HTTPSConnectionPool` in order to disable hostname verification (e.g., internal SSL certificates that do not supply a hostname).

It would be great if the parameter could be passed in without having to subclass. It may be worthwhile to also to support `assert_fingerprint` at the same time.

issue_title: setJointMotorControl2: Setting positions outside of the limits causes simulation issues
issue_body:
I am using setJointMotorControl2 using a simple script I adapted from one of yours.

```
#!/usr/bin/env python

import pybullet as p
import time
import math
import numpy as np

useRealTime = 0
fixedTimeStep = 0.001

physId = p.connect(p.SHARED_MEMORY)
if (physId<0):
	p.connect(p.GUI)

p.loadURDF("ground.urdf")
p.setGravity(0,0,-9.81)
p.setTimeStep(fixedTimeStep)

p.setRealTimeSimulation(0)
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/ant.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/point.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/half_cheetah.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/humanoid.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/humanoidstandup.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/inverted_double_pendulum.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/inverted_pendulum.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/humanoid_symmetric.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/hopper.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/reacher.xml")
# bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/swimmer.xml")
bodies = p.loadMJCF("mjcf/walker2d.xml")

minLimit = -math.pi/2
maxLimit = math.pi/2

p_gain = 0.8
speed = 1

# exclude = np.ones([len(bodies),50])
exclude = np.zeros([len(bodies),50])


exclude[1,4] = 0
exclude[1,5] = 0
exclude[1,8] = 1

for j in range(len(bodies)):
	for i in range(p.getNumJoints(bodies[j])):
		print "Setting [", j , "," , i , "]"
		p.setJointMotorControl2(bodies[j],i,p.TORQUE_CONTROL, 0)

t = 0
while True:
	print math.sin(t*speed)/2*(maxLimit-minLimit)
	for j in range(len(bodies)):
		for i in range(p.getNumJoints(bodies[j])):
			if exclude[j,i] == 1:
				print "Setting [", j , "," , i , "]"
				p.setJointMotorControl2(bodies[j],i,p.POSITION_CONTROL,math.sin(t*speed)/2*(maxLimit-minLimit),p_gain)
	p.stepSimulation()
	t = t + fixedTimeStep
```

Since not all joints are limited to -math.pi/2 math.pi/2, this will apply out of limits positions. Applying positions outside of the limits causes the whole multibody to fly around. Not sure if you want to do something about this. If I read the joint limits from file and apply only up to those, it worked very well (I experienced only some minor spasm as it overshoots the limit).

issue_title: Review docs on troubleshooting and correcting a stale JS build
issue_body:
- [ ] Review existing section in docs on the process of checkout and build of notebook including JS
- [ ] Create a section or FAQ for Troubleshooting/Tips
    * Q: What if my Notebook's user interface doesn't look as expected?
    * A: Check if you have run ``npm run build`` prior to ``jupyter notebook``.
    * Q: Is there a way to automate entering ``npm run build`` each time that I run new code updates

Feedback from @fperez about stale JS in issue #708 

> it was trying to run the notebook itself from a git master checkout. But I forgot to run npm run build, so my JS assets were out of date. And, not having stated either in my config or at the command line the ignore_minified_js=True flag, then I was getting stale JS assets.

> The behavior was thus rather confusing: at the command-line, a jupyter notebook --version call looked OK, since it reported a current version, which was indeed true. But that current version was 'half-current', if you will, since it was 'python-current', but 'javascript-old', as it was still running old JS code, from whenever I had last run a JS build.

> So, the summary is, you either:

>    get religious about always running npm run build

>    or you configure your setup with the ignore_minified_js=True flag.

issue_title: copy_column warranted?
issue_body:
As stated, I'm working on the implementation of a ORM rename_column to enable 2many and possibly property fields. While seems right now easy to rename a column to a manual one, which then gets initialized properly by the ORM at module loading time (so the plan), copying a column is slightly more complex (because construction and resolution of 2many table and columns, as well as data copying as the new field would initialize empty at first).
On the other hand the only use case of copy column, I can really think about is:

1. Copy a column
2. Do something with one of the columns, one functioning as source and one as destination)
3. source means basically "backup" in most cases, and in all cases means "inmutable"
4. destination means "trasnformation and storage for future use"

This can also be achieved in an ORM compatible way like this:
1. Rename column (ORM)
2. Let your model create the new column
3. map_values_orm 1 to 2

In this order of ideas, it could be argued, that copy could be deprecated in future version for the sake of uniformity and simplicity. 

What do you think in general and in detail?

issue_title: linting: Check reStructuredText markup in tool help tag
issue_body:
Galaxy will sometimes show a syntax error in the rendered tool ``<help>`` tag, e.g.

https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/commit/2c379779a47d6a5bc5993af6833f36cb6117c0c0

It would be nice for planemo linting to extract the ``<help>`` tag (after macro processing) and run it though an RST markup parser (ideally the same one used in Galaxy) to spot any errors.

issue_title: Doc release process is deprecated and broken
issue_body:
The upload_sphinx process for hosting docs with pythonhosted.org has been deprecated and now seems to be broken.  I've removed it for the time being from the release process, but this means that the docs will continue to fall out of date.

We need to replace the docs release process with [RTD](https://readthedocs.org/)

issue_title: On Python 3, __class__ can be used without self
issue_body:
*Original report by [icordasc](https://launchpad.net/~icordasc) (@sigmavirus24?) on [Launchpad](https://bugs.launchpad.net/bugs/1487725):*

------------------------------------

On Python 3, the following code raises a warning:

```python
class Test(object):
    def __init__(self):
        print(__class__.__name__)
        self.x = 1

t = Test()
```

This is actually valid Python 3 code, but PyFlakes doesn't quite understand that:

    test.py:3: undefined name '__class__'

issue_title: Add license installation

Code snippet for the revoking the license:
```python
mgnt = ManagementRoot(bigip_mgnt, user, pass)
mgnt.tm.sys.license.exec_cmd('revoke')`
```
Code snippet for the license installation:
```python
mgnt = ManagementRoot(bigip_mgnt, user, pass)
mgnt.tm.sys.license.exec_cmd('install', registrationKey=regkey)
```

issue_title: PhoneNumberMixin forces field name and can't be used with two fields
issue_body:
The current implementation of the `PhoneNumberMixin` makes it impossible to customise the field name. It also is impossible to use it with more than one phone number field. 

We should investigate if we can instead specify field name(s) as an attribute, or convert it into a custom form field.

issue_title: commit offset failed with  No offset stored
issue_body:
Description
===========
I create a new topic and try to commit offset on it with three different values: -2, -1, 99, but only 99 worked, others occurred 'Commit failed: Local: No offset stored'。I'm confused on it, could someone help me on this?

confluent-kafka-python: ('0.11.0', 720896)
libversion:  ('0.11.3', 721919)
kafka : 0.10.2.1


log:
`
RESET offset to -2 for topic topic.sucl.new(0) 
commit offsets: [TopicPartition{topic=topic.sucl.new,partition=0,offset=-2,error=None}]
COMMIT: commit offset failed with message: _NO_OFFSET(-168) >> Commit failed: Local: No offset stored
`
`
RESET offset to -1 for topic topic.sucl.new(0)
commit offsets: [TopicPartition{topic=topic.sucl.new,partition=0,offset=-1,error=None}]
COMMIT: commit offset failed with message: _NO_OFFSET(-168) >> Commit failed: Local: No offset stored
`
`
RESET offset to 99 for topic topic.sucl.new(0)
commit offsets: [TopicPartition{topic=topic.sucl.new,partition=0,offset=99,error=None}]
`


How to reproduce
================
`

    client = Test(
                    hosts='xxx:9093',
                    ssl_path=xxxx,
                    passwd=None,
                    logger=log.LogHandler('test', path='.', log_level='DEBUG').logger,
                   new_version=True)

    client.reset_offset({'topic.sucl.new': -2})
    client.reset_offset({'topic.sucl.new': -1})
    client.reset_offset({'topic.sucl.new': 99})
`

definition of class Test
`
class Test():

    def __init__(self, hosts, ssl_path, passwd, new_version, logger):
        self._cfg = {
            'group.id': 'group.sucl',
            'bootstrap.servers': hosts,
            'log.connection.close': False,
            'security.protocol': 'ssl' if ssl_path else 'plaintext',
            'ssl.key.location': os.path.join(ssl_path, 'client.key.unsecure') if ssl_path else None,
            'ssl.ca.location': os.path.join(ssl_path, 'ca-cert') if ssl_path else None,
            'ssl.certificate.location': os.path.join(ssl_path, 'client.crt') if ssl_path else None,
            'ssl.key.password': passwd,
            'api.version.request': True if new_version else False,
            'broker.version.fallback': '0.10.2.1' if new_version else '0.9.0',
            'log_level': 6,
            'message.max.bytes': 1000000,
            'log.connection.close': True if new_version else False,
            # 'debug':'generic,broker,topic,metadata',
            'enable.auto.commit': False,
            'auto.commit.interval.ms': 1000,
            'fetch.min.bytes': 1024 * 1024,
            'fetch.wait.max.ms': 1000,
            'fetch.message.max.bytes': 1048576,
            'default.topic.config': {
                'auto.offset.reset': 'smallest',
            },
        }
        self._consumer = Consumer(**self._cfg)
        self._logger = logger

    def reset_offset(self, offsets):
        def get_value(para):
            if para in ('min', 'max'):
                return confluent_kafka.OFFSET_BEGINNING if para == 'min' \
                                                        else confluent_kafka.OFFSET_END
            elif isinstance(para, int):
                return para

            self._logger.warning('unknown reset value: {}, will not reset offset'.format(para))
            return None

        if isinstance(offsets, dict):
            assigns = [TopicPartition(_topic, 0, get_value(_offset)) for _topic, _offset in offsets.items()
                       if get_value(_offset)]
        elif isinstance(offsets, list):
            assigns = [TopicPartition(_item[0], _item[1], get_value(_item[2])) for _item in offsets
                       if get_value(_item[2])]
        else:
            self._logger.warning('unknown type: {} for param[offsets], will not reset offset'.format(type(offsets).__name__))
            return None

        [self._logger.warning('RESET offset to {0} for topic {1}({2})'.format(_t.offset, _t.topic, _t.partition))
         for _t in assigns]

        return self.commit(assigns, if_format=True)

    def commit(self, offsets=None, if_format=False):
        if if_format:
            topics = offsets
        else:
            src = self._end_offset if offsets is None else offsets
            topics = [TopicPartition(i, k, m) for i, j in src.items() for k, m in j.items()]

        for _ in range(3):
            try:
                self._consumer.commit(offsets=topics, async=False)
                return True
            except (confluent_kafka.KafkaException,) as e:
                _exp_name, exp_code = e.args[0].name(), e.args[0].code()
                if int(exp_code) == 27:  # REBALANCE_IN_PROGRESS
                    self._logger.warning('COMMIT: kafka server is now in rebalancing, will retry...')
                    time.sleep(1)
                    continue
                self._logger.error('COMMIT: commit offset failed with message: {0}({1}) >> {2}'.format(
                    e.args[0].name(), e.args[0].code(), e.args[0].str()
                ))
                return False

        self._logger.error('COMMIT: commit failed after 3 times retry')
        return False

`



Checklist
=========
Please provide the following information:

 - [√] confluent-kafka-python and librdkafka version (`confluent_kafka.version()` and `confluent_kafka.libversion()`):
 - [√ ] Apache Kafka broker version:
 - [√] Client configuration: `{...}`
 - [ ] Operating system:
 - [√ ] Provide client logs (with `'debug': '..'` as necessary)
 - [ ] Provide broker log excerpts
 - [ ] Critical issue

issue_title: All Base maps loading at the same time
issue_body:
I'm not sure If this is a bug in Leaflet or Folium, but if you add a bunch of tile layers and use a layer control it will cause every tile layer to be active at the same time and download tiles for every layer. As soon as you select a new layer it starts loading tiles from only that layer like it should.

```Python
import folium

m = folium.Map(location=[44, -73], zoom_start=3, tiles=None)
folium.TileLayer('openstreetmap').add_to(m)
folium.TileLayer('stamentoner').add_to(m)
folium.TileLayer('stamenTerrain').add_to(m)
folium.TileLayer('stamenwatercolor').add_to(m)
folium.TileLayer('cartodbpositron').add_to(m)
folium.TileLayer('cartodbdark_matter').add_to(m)
folium.LayerControl().add_to(m)

m.save('map.html')
```

issue_title: Tmux right side only visible for first 3 windows
issue_body: When I first start tmux, with no prior sessions, the right side of the status bar appears as normal (with uptime, time, server name, etc.). However, after I add three additional windows (for a total of four), the Powerline data changes as follows: * If `powerline-daemon` **is** running, then the right side of the status bar changes to the first line of the powerline-daemon usage message: `usage: powerline-daemon [-h] [-r MODULE] [-w WIDTH] [--last-exit-code INT]`. * If `powerline-daemon` **is not** running, then the right side of the status bar becomes blank. If I kill or start powerline-daemon while inside a tmux session, the status bar will adjust to the corresponding appearance after a moment. This is not an issue of *how many* tmux windows I have. If, after creating new windows, I switch back to one of the first three windows, the status bar goes back to normal. Switching back to window 4+, the status bar goes wrong again. This is not an issue of the window number. If I destroy window 1 (where the status bar was correct), and then create another window, that new window will be numbered 1, but the status bar won't be correct for it. I suspect this is because the "pane ID" always increases; although window numbers get re-used, pane IDs do not. The tmux window count applies across sessions, so if I "use up" the correctly working windows in one session, disconnect from that session, and create a new session, *all* the windows in the new session will have the wrong status bar. Killing the tmux server (as by exiting all sessions) and restarting it makes new windows have the right status bar, but only the first three windows again. If I run the tmux command `run-shell 'powerline tmux right'`, the output appears correct. Likewise if I run env "$POWERLINE_COMMAND" $POWERLINE_COMMAND_ARGS tmux right -R pane_id=\`tmux display -p "#D"\`, which is what's configured in *bindings/tmux/powerline-base.conf*. (In my environment, `POWERLINE_COMMAND` is simply `powerline`, and `POWERLINE_COMMAND_ARGS`.) I'm using Powerline 2.2 installed with `pip3 install powerline-status` under Python 3.4 and Tmux 2.0. What else can I do to investigate this problem? --- Want to back this issue? **[Post a bounty on it!](https://www.bountysource.com/issues/24395018-tmux-right-side-only-visible-for-first-3-windows?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F283196&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github)** We accept bounties via [Bountysource](https://www.bountysource.com/?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F283196&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github).

issue_title: errno not consistently set when raising SerialException
issue_body:
Occasionally, when a `SerialException` is raised it does not contain an `errno`, instead it is stringified and passed as the `message` of the exception.

This makes the calling code less elegant because it has to try to parse the text of the error in order to figure out what happened.

Here is an example: https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/blob/master/serial/serialposix.py#L508

And another one, where an `errno` is passed: https://github.com/pyserial/pyserial/blob/master/serial/serialposix.py#L268

issue_title: ABC Data format for multiple licenses in ABOUT file
issue_body:
The current ABOUT files treat multiple licenses in string block
```
license_name: |
    Apache License 2.0
    Oasis WS Security Specification License
    W3C Software Notice and License
    WS-Policy Specification
license_file: |
    apache-2.0.LICENSE
    oasis-ws-security-spec.LICENSE
    w3c.LICENSE
    ws-policy-specification.LICENSE
license_url: |
    http://spgallery.arrs.arrisi.com/urn/?urn=urn:dje:license:apache-2.0
    http://spgallery.arrs.arrisi.com/urn/?urn=urn:dje:license:oasis-ws-security-spec
    http://spgallery.arrs.arrisi.com/urn/?urn=urn:dje:license:w3c
    http://spgallery.arrs.arrisi.com/urn/?urn=urn:dje:license:ws-policy-specification
etc...
```
However, in the ABC Data format, we should use a list instead.
For instance,
```
licenses:
     - key: apache-2.0
       name: Apache License 2.0
       file: apache-2.0.LICENSE
       url: http://spgallery.arrs.arrisi.com/urn/?urn=urn:dje:license:apache-2.0

     - key: oasis-ws-security-spec
       name: Oasis WS Security Specification License
       file: oasis-ws-security-spec.LICENSE
       url: http://spgallery.arrs.arrisi.com/urn/?urn=urn:dje:license:oasis-ws-security-spec
....
```

issue_body:
```
File "lib/python3.5/site-packages/license_expression.py", line 324, in build
    if isinstance(expression, basestring) and expression.strip():
NameError: name 'basestring' is not defined
```

Could be solved with the following:
```
try:
    unicode = unicode
except NameError:
    str = str
    unicode = str
    bytes = bytes
    basestring = (str, bytes)

    str = str
    unicode = unicode
    bytes = str
    basestring = basestring

issue_title: Django 1.8 compatibility
issue_body:
Running Django 1.8 and Django-Tastypie 0.12.1 on Windows, Python 2.7, running `manage.py check` fails with this error:
```
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\tastypie\resources.py", line 2210, in Base
ModelResource
    @transaction.commit_on_success()
AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'commit_on_success'
```
The decorator was removed in this version of Django, so says Django documentation

> These features have reached the end of their deprecation cycle and so have been removed in Django 1.8 (please see the deprecation timeline for more details):
> ...
> * the decorators and context managers `autocommit`, `commit_on_success`, and `commit_manually`, defined in django.db.transaction
> ...

issue_title: scm-source sanity check for mark trusted
issue_body:
Marking an image trusted shall be only possible under these conditions:

- is scm-source info present
- is it syntactically correct, according to https://stups.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user-guide/application-development.html#scm-source-json
- is url a valid git url?
- is revision a full commit hash (not an abbreviated one)
- git status is clean (empty)

issue_title: Cheap simplification option?
issue_body:
Hi,

when using the optimizing solver, the calls to `upper`/`lower` may return AST objects with `epsilon` formula inside. I get rid of those by performing substitution followed by the simplification (assuming I know what epsilon can be safely replaced by) in order to be able to convert it to number by using `get_numeral_string`.
Yet that has some disadvantages: Z3 default simplification is rather heavy-weight, and can be interrupted.
Is there possibly simpler and lighter approach? Could be my best bet simply turning off all (almost all) options inside the `simplify` call

issue_title: (set-logic ALL) only partially disables builtin List sort
issue_body:
The following example produces an assertion failure:
```smt2
(set-logic ALL)
(declare-datatypes ((List 0)) (((Nil) (Cons (head Int) (tail List)))))
(define-fun-rec f ((n Int) (l List)) Bool
  (or (and ((_ is Nil) l))
      (and ((_ is Cons) l)
           (f (- n 1) (tail l)))))
(check-sat)
```

```
ASSERTION VIOLATION
File: /home/moritz/code/z3/src/ast/datatype_decl_plugin.cpp
Line: 39
n == get_def().params().size()
(C)ontinue, (A)bort, (S)top, (T)hrow exception, Invoke (G)DB
```

I took a look at the source and it looks like the decision on whether to create the builtin list sort is made in [`init_manager_core`](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/f22abaa7135f46953211fc304aa51e938e0d0948/src/cmd_context/cmd_context.cpp#L721). This function is apparently called twice, the first time `new_manager` is `true` and at `m_logic` is set to `ALL` so the list sort is not created. The second time `new_manager` is `false` but now `m_logic` is a null pointer so we create the list leading to the error.

Sadly, I know too little about the code to know what the right solution is or why `m_logic` is set to a null pointer the second time.

issue_title: conditional display for columns
issue_body:
Columns should be made to conditionally displayed through permissions, app settings, etc. We could do a ConditionalColumn, but I'd rather be able to simply pass a lambda to a Column of any type to determine whether it would display.

Should also look at `render_in` and make sure it can handle lambdas.

issue_title: CNN-LSTM
issue_body:
Hi,

I am implementing a CNN-LSTM, but I was struggling when I was trying to reshape the output from CNN to be in the input shape required by LSTM. See below.

```
network = lasagne.layers.InputLayer(
    shape=(None, n_channels, height, width),
    input_var=input_var
)
network = lasagne.layers.dnn.Conv2DDNNLayer(
    network,
    num_filters=40,
    filter_size=(4, 4),
    stride=(2, 2),
    nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.rectify,
    W=lasagne.init.GlorotUniform()
)
network = lasagne.layers.dnn.MaxPool2DDNNLayer(
    network,
    pool_size=(2, 2)
)

### Reshape input into the shape (batch_size, sequence_length, num_inputs) ###

network = lasagne.layers.LSTMLayer(
    network, 
    n_hidden,
    nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.tanh
)
network = ReshapeLayer(
    network, 
    (-1, 250)
)
network = lasagne.layers.DenseLayer(
    lasagne.layers.dropout(network, p=.5),
    num_units=250,
    nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.rectify
)
network = lasagne.layers.DenseLayer(
    lasagne.layers.dropout(network, p=.5),
    num_units=2,
    nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.softmax
)
```

As CNN receive each input at a time, how can I design the ``network`` so that LSTM receives input with ``sequence_length=5``?

Thanks

issue_title: Error running Commuter: Cannot find module "react-hot-loader"
issue_body:
Using docker for ease of reproducing the error. Here's the current Dockerfile I'm running

```
FROM node:latest

USER node

# resolving permission errors
RUN mkdir /home/node/.npm-global
ENV PATH=/home/node/.npm-global/bin:$PATH
ENV NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=/home/node/.npm-global

# attempted to fix: Cannot find module "react-hot-loader"
RUN npm install react react-hot-loader @nteract/commuter -g

WORKDIR /app
ENTRYPOINT commuter
```

The first error I hit is discussed and resolved [here](https://github.com/nodejs/node-gyp/issues/1236#issuecomment-309447800) related to permission errors from `node-gyp`. 

I was then able to install with `RUN npm install @nteract/commuter -g` and build the image with `docker build -t commuter .` But when running the container `docker run -p 4000:4000 -v /local/notebook/path:/app commuter` the UI flashes the expected result and then displays only a large `200 | OK.` in the middle of the screen. The browser console reveals `Error: Cannot find module "react-hot-loader"`.  Including `react-hot-loader` in the install then leads to `Cannot find module react`, add react in to the install and then back to cannot find react-hot-loader.

issue_title: nightly .deb package archive
issue_body:
I'm in the process of setting up a debian package archive that allows installing nteract with `apt-get install` on linux distros such as Ubuntu. Also automatic updates.

- [ ] I'd like to make nightly packages from the master branch on git available. Does nteract have a culture of merging mostly working code to master (no point in nightly builds if not)? From personal experience, I think yes.

My current thinking:

- `git pull` latest master branch
- `git checkout -b new-package-branch`
- [ ] ~~~`npm version patch`? The version number needs to change, but the git tags could be thrown away. This would mean that actual named releases here on github would need to be for example `npm version minor` instead of `patch` like now.~~~  Actually appending the date as -yyyymmdd to whatever the current version number is probably a better solution, since it will be correct semantic versioning, which is required by the packaging system. It would also help with tying the version number back to the version in git. 
- [x] `npm run dist` to create the .deb package
- [ ] copy the .deb to the archive. Debian packages archives are just any ftp/http hosts serving static files. I haven't quite figured out the best way to host all the files.
- [x] update archive metadata and sign it.
- [x] packages are available via regular system updates

---

There is launchpad.net, which is commonly used to host personal package archives, but as far as I'm aware the `electron-builder` process in its current is not compatible with the build process on launchpad. 

I'm trying to figure out if there is a way to use the GitHub release mechanism to host the `.deb`s for the archive (not for nightly builds, but proper builds maybe). I'm pretty sure the answer is yes. If github pages had http redirects you could even host the metadata and have a repo-contained package archive. That could be fun.

---

I've been playing around with this stuff at https://github.com/jarmokivekas/nteract-apt-archive

issue_title: Document core and client API
Importing CSV to sqlite3 fails due to invalid command-line syntax
issue_body:
When importing a CSV odo invokes sqlite3 with command-line arguments: `sqlite3 -nullvalue '' -separator , -cmd '.import "/path/to/csv/file" tablename' /path/to/db/file`

However, current sqlite3 (version 3.6.20) does not support a `-cmd` option. Instead, the invocation should look like this: `sqlite3 -nullvalue '' -separator , /path/to/db/file '.import "/path/to/csv/file" tablename'`

I patched odo/backends/sql_csv.py:compile_from_csv_sqlite() accordingly and it works like a charm. Happy to submit the patch if that'll speed things up. Please advise, thanks!

issue_title: s3 connection fails in Frankfurt region due to missing host argument
issue_body:
There is a known issue with Boto in which connections to Frankfurt require SigV4 which in turn requires a 'host' parameter:

BotoClientError: When using SigV4, you must specify a 'host' parameter.
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/26744712/s3-using-boto-and-sigv4-missing-host-parameter

When connecting manually with boto I can make it work by providing the host explicitly:
boto.connect_s3(host='s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com')

However, in Odo I don't have the ability to do this as the connect call is buried.  Unfortunately I don't think it's possible to fix this in a boto config file.  I was able to get it work by patching odo.backends.aws.get_s3_connection locally and hardcoding the host parameter which is not ideal.

issue_title: Add ability to control wait_ready aarguements across threads
issue_body:
Request to be able to pass arguments to the wait_ready() function within to reduce hang time and enable graceful failure.

This introduces a place where exceptions could be raised and does not allow for adjusting the timeout inside certain commands which are threaded and face the default parameters.  It would be nice to be able to specify a timeout and not have it raise an exception if something fails inside this function which can break code.  There are multiple instances where there is a wait_ready inside commands to block them and allow for things to finish which is understandable.  If there is a weak connection, these commands may see corruption, get hung and timeout after the default 30 seconds as opposed to timing out after a defined period, returning False and then being called again multiple times within the default 30 seconds until success which would see a higher success rate.  This in my opinion is a safety risk as it removes the ability for one to control their own timeouts and does not allow graceful failure.  

example:
`
dronekit-python / dronekit / __init__.py :

2694         def download(self): 
2695         ''' 
2696         Download all waypoints from the vehicle. 
2697         The download is asynchronous. Use :py:func:`wait_ready()` to block your thread until the download is complete. 
2698         ''' 
2699         self.wait_ready() 
`

Possible solution path: Add a parameter, global or environment variable for timeout and whether to include raise_exception=False to the default wait_ready command so you can set the default arguments once and it will carry across all threads.  This would remove the necessity of modifying and possibly hurting backwards compatibility of code.  

Rationale: 30 seconds is too long for a default timeout on most modern communication systems and exceptions are not required and introduce more code to handle them.  The 30 second hang is too long and could lead to loss of control for a long duration due to one packet being corrupted.

issue_title: Insufficiently enthusiastic about emitting header table size shrinkages.
issue_body:
Whenever we shrink the table size in the encoder, we *must* emit a header table size update field. This is true even if we change the header table size multiple times: e.g., we shrink and then raise the header table size. Currently we don't do this, which means we're technically violating the RFC.

This came from the following comment on the ML:

> - If between headers decoder reduces the limit below size signaled by
>  encoder, the encoder must first reduce the table size to the minimum
>  it was between the frames or less (it can then increase it up to
>  current limit).
> 
> As example of the last point:
> ```
> [4k dynamic table size in use]
> --> HEADERS
> <-- SETTINGS(SETTINGS_HEADER_TABLE_SIZE=4k)
> <-- SETTINGS(SETTINGS_HEADER_TABLE_SIZE=2k)
> <-- SETTINGS(SETTINGS_HEADER_TABLE_SIZE=4k)
> <-- SETTINGS(SETTINGS_HEADER_TABLE_SIZE=8k)
> <-- SETTINGS(SETTINGS_HEADER_TABLE_SIZE=6k)
> --> HEADERS
> ```
>  
> The second HEADERS must first reduce the dynamic table to at most
> 2k. It can then increase dynamic table size to up to 6k.

issue_title: Finer grain for permissions
issue_body:
As per discussion with @chrismbeckett on IRC:

In business application, having write permissions doesn't means nor that you can delete the data nor that you can change its permissions.

We should probably let people define another sharable model for their Kinto Instance.

Today we have:

 - ProtectedResource
 - SharableResource

We may want to let people define other ones more complex with their own inheritance tree and another set of permissions or at least let them define which verb are link to which permission.

Keep in mind that without a collection schema it is possible to remove a records by putting an empty record instead, with the collection schema it is not possible to remove something by putting an empty record for instance but it is still possible to override it with wrong data.

Maybe we should investigate this use case and see what are the options to handle that.

issue_title: Download PNG is totally blank using tensorboard 1.5.0
issue_body:
## Issue description

Image generated on "Download PNG" is totally blank with tensorboard 1.5.0.

Image generated on "Download PNG" is fine after replaced tensorboard 1.5.0 with tensorboard 0.4.0.

## Environment

- PC 1 (GPU version): Windows 10 + Python 3.6.3 + TensorFlow-GPU 1.5.0 + Tensorboard 1.5.0 + CUDA 9.0 + cuDNN 7.0.5 
- PC 2 (CPU version): Windows 7 + Python 3.6.3 + TensorFlow 1.5.0 + Tensorboard 1.5.0
- PC 3 (CPU version): Windows 7 + Python 3.6.3 + TensorFlow 1.5.0 + Tensorboard 1.5.0

> Not tested on Linux yet.

## Issue Steps

1. Install python 3.6.3, tensorflow 1.5.0 (GPU version or CPU version).

> Dependency package tensorboard 1.5.0 will be installed with tensorflow 1.5.0.

2. Generate a log, such as the code below:

```
import tensorflow as tf

sess = tf.InteractiveSession()

with tf.name_scope('a'):
  a = tf.Variable(4, dtype=tf.int32)
with tf.name_scope('b'):
  b = tf.Variable(3, dtype=tf.int32)
with tf.name_scope('c'):
  c = tf.multiply(a, b)
tf.summary.scalar('output', c)

merged = tf.summary.merge_all()
summary, c_val = sess.run([merged, c], feed_dict={a: 4, b: 5})
writer = tf.summary.FileWriter(r"C:\tmp", graph=sess.graph)
writer.add_summary(summary)
writer.close()
```

3. Run `tensorboard --logdir=C:\tmp`, then open tensorboard (port 6006) in Chrome browser.

4. Click `Download PNG` on the left sidebar of `TensorBoard` - `GRAPHS`.

- File name: `graph_large_attrs_key=_too_large_attrs&limit_attr_size=1024&run=.png`
- File size: `3.06 KB`
- File pixel: 495 * 286

5. Open the image, but the image shows nothing invalid. The image is totally blank.

## Recovery Steps

1. Download "tensorflow_tensorboard-0.4.0-py3-none-any.whl" on [PYPI](https://testpypi.python.org/pypi/tensorflow-tensorboard/0.4.0)

2. `pip3 install tensorflow_tensorboard-0.4.0-py3-none-any.whl`. After installation tensorboard 0.4.0, the tensorflow version is still 1.5.0.

3. **Use the same log in Issue Step 2**. Run `tensorboard --logdir=C:\tmp`, then open tensorboard (port 6006) in Chrome browser.

4. Click `Download PNG` on the left sidebar of `TensorBoard` - `GRAPHS`.

- File name: `graph_large_attrs_key=_too_large_attrs&limit_attr_size=1024&run=D%5C.png`
- File size: `11.9 KB`
- File pixel: `495 * 286`

5. Open the image, the image shows graph successfully.

issue_title: Add support for setup.py
Limitar los títulos de los datasets a 100 caracteres
issue_body:
**Contexto**

La API de CKAN da error al intentar federar un dataset cuyo título tiene más de 100 caracteres, si bien esto sí se permite desde la experiencia visual (una de las rarezas de CKAN).

No nos parece mal, de todas maneras, ajustarnos a este límite para tener títulos de datasets más concisos.

**Propuesta**

Esta validación es _muy sencilla de agregar_ se hace desde el _schema_ en JSON que lee la librería `jsonschema`.

En la línea de configuración de la propiedad `title` del esquema `dataset` (https://github.com/datosgobar/pydatajson/blob/master/pydatajson/schemas/dataset.json#L35) probablemente haya que agregar un par clave/valor nuevo que limita la cantidad de caracteres del campo a 100.

Aprovecho este issue sencillo para que @lrromero se familiarice con la librería de validación: https://python-jsonschema.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

issue_title: crash in publishEvent with data containing non-json serializable fields
issue_body:
I've been writing a custom encoder to be able to send messages to watson iot in a custom binary format.

Line 166 in device.py does json.dumps(data) before running the data through the encoder. This means that the data you send to watson must be json serializable even if you have your own encoder that does not use json at all.

This behaviour seems undesirable, there are a two fixes i can think of:

1. replace json.dumps(data) with str(data). Smallest possible fix but most objects only print "<file.class at 0x00...>"
2. move the log statement down to line 171 and log str(payload). Works for json encoders but has the same problem as point 1 for other payload types. 

If there is a consensus on the desired behaviour I will create a pull request with the fix

issue_title: Enable crowdsourced video caption creation
issue_body:
Having captions for all the videos would improve both the searchability and the accessibility of the site.  Doing it all at once would be a huge project, but encouraging site users to submit captions could make it possible to gradually get them all done.  MIT has a [captioning resources page](https://ux.mit.edu/accessibility/captions) that looks pretty useful.  It mentions [Amara](http://www.amara.org/en/), which is in use on pyvideo (but seems to have some [issues](https://github.com/pyvideo/richard/issues/284), at least as currently implemented).

We should at least make sure we allow usage of whatever Amara and YouTube captions and/or PyCon US live captioning already exists.

issue_title: Data download progress bar
issue_body:
This may be very trivial, but while downloading CIFAR-10 using cifar_10.py, I thought it was stuck. Turns out that it was just download taking a bit of a time. In hindsight, I should've known how big the dataset was before downloading(~170MB), but maybe it's nice to know the progress, especially for bigger datasets. Again, this looks very trivial to me so let me know if you don't need to see this kind of issues.

so in datasets/cifar_10.py I added
```python
#reporthook from stackoverflow #13881092
def reporthook(blocknum, blocksize, totalsize):
    readsofar = blocknum * blocksize
    if totalsize > 0:
        percent = readsofar * 1e2 / totalsize
        s = "\r%5.1f%% %*d / %d" % (
            percent, len(str(totalsize)), readsofar, totalsize)
        sys.stderr.write(s)
        if readsofar >= totalsize: # near the end
            sys.stderr.write("\n")
    else: # total size is unknown
        sys.stderr.write("read %d\n" % (readsofar,))
```
and changed
```python
filepath, _ = urllib.request.urlretrieve(source_url + filename, filepath)
```
to
```python
filepath, _ = urllib.request.urlretrieve(source_url + filename, filepath,reporthook)
```

Note: There is a slight discrepacny between the numbers when complete
```
Downloading CIFAR 10, Please wait...
100.0% 170500096 / 170498071
('Succesfully downloaded', 'cifar-10-python.tar.gz', 170498071, 'bytes.')
```

issue_title: Representing P most frequent words without n-grams
issue_body:
Quoting from the last paragraph of section 3 in the [word representation paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1607.04606v1.pdf) - 

`To improve the efficiency of our model, we do not use n-grams to represent the P most frequent words in the vocabulary. There is a trade-off in the choice of P, as smaller values imply higher computational cost but better performance.`

I couldn't find any further references to it in the implementation details or the released code. I was looking to use this to directly imitate the original skipgram model. Was this dropped from the final implementation? 

The best alternate (hack-y) way to do this seems to modify the computeNgrams method in Dictionary. Would simply making it do nothing be a correct approach?

Sorry for raising this as an issue, wasn't sure where else to ask this.

issue_title: Embedded Pipelines Raise AttributeError on .fit()
issue_body:
If I create a "hierarchical pipeline" ( a pipeline where one step is another pipeline), then the Pipeline will raise an AttributeError on .fit() because it reads the imblearn.pipeline.Pipeline object as having a .fit_transform() attribute, and thus sending it to .fit_transform() where it tries to call .fit_transform() on an imblearn object within one of the steps. 

The following will reproduce the error:
```

from imblearn.over_sampling import SMOTE
from sklearn.datasets import make_classification
from sklearn import preprocessing
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier

X, y = make_classification(n_classes=2, class_sep=2, weights=[0.1, 0.9],
    n_informative=3, n_redundant=1, flip_y=0,
    n_features=20, n_clusters_per_class=1,
    n_samples=1000, random_state=10)

Pipeline(steps=[
('(std->smt)', Pipeline(steps=[
    ('std', preprocessing.StandardScaler(copy=True, with_mean=True, with_std=True)), 
    ('smt', SMOTE(k=5, kind='regular', m=10, n_jobs=-1, out_step=0.5, random_state=None,     ratio='auto'))
    ])), 
('knn', KNeighborsClassifier(algorithm='auto', leaf_size=30, metric='minkowski',
           metric_params=None, n_jobs=1, n_neighbors=5, p=2,
           weights='uniform'))
]).fit(X,y)

```

issue_title: Set up Transifex
Reduce latency of RevealSecret and SecretRequest messages
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The RevealSecret and SecretRequest messages don't need to be ordered, this is fine because neither message contain a balance proof (nonce), so the validity of these messages don't depend on the order in which they arrive at the target.

Currently the messages are ordered, using an anonymous queue per recipient address, which adds unnecessary latency for these messages, this latency is shared among all channels with the recipient node.

## Solution

Introduce a new retry mechanism for unordered messages retries, i.e. that doesn't rely on a queue, and use it for sending these messages.

## Related

Collapse multiple raiden messages within a single transport packet. #309

issue_title: Implement a `no_wait` feature for actions and nested workflows
issue_body:
SoS nested workflow executes sequentially. It makes it hard to parallel scenarios like: 

```
sos_run('A+B+C')
sos_run('A+D+C')
```
where B and D can in principle execute at the same time as long as A completes. Currently the only way to make it happen is to write the script in `Make` style. Here we propose:

```
sos_run('A+B+C', no_wait = True)
sos_run('A+D+C', no_wait = True)
```
and for actions 

```
run: no_wait = True
...

python: no_wait = True
```

For nested workflow this will result in consolidating these sequences to the master DAG before execution. For actions this will result in no_wait actions to be sent to background for execution while the executor takes in the next action in line.

from sklearn.metrics import f1_score

def f1(preds, targs, start=0.17, end=0.24, step=0.01):

Michael

issue_title: ImageFolder Dataset: `make_dataset` with the same (fixed) order.
issue_body:
When creating the dataset using `ImageFolder`, different machines can have different orders of images. 

This may not be an issue for the training set, but when I want to compare the validation results among different machines (especially when I wanted to ensemble the softmax outputs of validation set), different orders of images can be annoying.

A simple modification can solve the problem: currently the `make_dataset` function use `for target in os.listdir(dir):`, we can simply change it to `for target in sorted(os.listdir(dir)):`.

Since the loading procedure can be shuffled, I have not found creating the dataset with a sorted order have any drawbacks. Actually, it will be much easier for the users when they want to ensemble the outputs of the full dataset among different machines (where `os.listdir(dir)` might produce different orders of results`).

issue_title: find() / findall() do client-side filtering and could be optimized
issue_body:
The implementations of `find()` and `findall()` in `BaseResourceManager` retrieve all objects and then apply the specified filtering. However, the HMC supports server-side filtering by means of specifying query parameters on the URL.

In order to optimize performance, I suggest to change this to exploit the server-side filtering.

One option to implement this could be to add the filtering capability to `list()` and then to exploit it in `find()` and `findall()`.

issue_title: ray.get memory is "slow"
issue_body:
TLDR; There's something about memory allocated by ray.get that makes it hard to get that data into PyTorch/TensorFlow fast enough. Numpy arrays can take 9-12ms to ingest, but numpy arrays coming from Ray take 45ms

Deduping from [discussion](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/ray-dev/OWOrmalTxro) in ray-dev to take advantage of post 1990s word-editing technology.

To get a scale of ideal performance, consider that V100 computes Resnet-50 gradient in 100ms for batch 32, and this gradient is about 100MB in size. Waiting for 45 ms to get Ray parameter values into the framework would drop GPU utilization by 30%

https://github.com/diux-dev/cluster/blob/master/yuxin_numpy/tf_numpy_benchmark.py

issue_title: Ustream streams freeze after 5 seconds or so
What should we do with money people donate to the team via Bountysource or other tools (as oppossed to individuals)?
issue_body:
### Description

A few days ago @liamkennedy very generously donated $120 USD to the bountysource (thanks Liam!). As this is the first ever team donation I'd like to discuss how we want to handle this. Currently only myself and @beardypig are marked as bounty hunters for the project, and I'm the only actual member of the project. I see a few potential options and if anyone else has other ideas please voice them here:

1) We cash out as BTC and determine some kind of split for those interested.
2) We leave the money in the Bountysorce account in the event we want to post a bounty on a specific issue.
3) We set a default bounty for many issues and if people do them they can get paid out.
4) We cash out as USD/BTC and donate to other projects or charities.

Between option 1 and option 2 I prefer 2 simply because I don't know how we would determine a split that everyone thinks is fair, plus we have no way to pay out users like @melmorabity or @cdrage or a host of other users who have contributed and it seems like there's potential for it to be a big hassle or for people to feel unfairly compensated since it's a team donation as opposed to a direct donation.

Option 3 is okay, but it just adds more management work as I don't think you can set a default value for all issues.

Option 4 is also a possibility, but seems kind of stupid to me because if we do this Liam just lost out on his tax deduction for donating to an org that actually counts as a charitable donation.

Anyways just wanted to bring this up for some discussion. @liamkennedy I'm assuming you donated to bountysource since most of our donation methods were kind of mediocre or hard to do via PayPal, did you have a specific way you wanted things to be split? If so let me know and I'll cash it out as BTC and pay out to people.

Looking forward to everyone's input!

issue_title: Auto detect Twitch (and other services?) VODs and enable the --player-passthrough hls option when using them
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [ ] This is a bug report.
- [ ] This is a plugin request.
- [x] This is a feature request.
- [x] I used the search function to find already opened/closed issues or pull requests.

### Description

I use Streamlink to watch Twitch VODs as well as live streams, but it's a bit tiring to always have to include the (quite long) `--player-passthrough hls` option to enable seeking through the VOD.  
I'm not sure if there's any use case for watching Twitch VODs with the default options (though I'd be happy to learn of reasons why).

Because of this I suggest auto-detecting Twitch VOD URLs and enabling the `--player-passthrough hls` option automatically. Usually when I introduced Livestreamer (and now Streamlink) to friends with less experience with the command line (and even less with reading documentation), it would be kind of awkward to explain that an extra option was needed to enable what is to the user basic functionality when watching VODs. It would streamline the user experience not to have to do that.

### Reproduction steps / Stream URLs to test

1. Run `streamlink [Twitch VOD url]` - can't seek
2. Run `streamlink [Twitch VOD url] --player-passthrough hls` - can seek

issue_title: Clarification about on_delete in Django 2.0
issue_body:
Will `ParentalKey` require the `on_delete` argument to be set when Django 2.0 support is implemented? Reflecting the requirement for `ForeignKey`?

It might be good to clarify the default in the readme if it is not required and will continue to default to `models.CASCADE`. Also, this might be a factor to consider for Django 2.0 support in general.

**Context**

Django 2.0 will require `on_delete` to be set for ForeignKey.

> A many-to-one relationship. Requires two positional arguments: the class to which the model is related and the on_delete option.
https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/ref/models/fields/#foreignkey

Django 1.11 did not require it, and set it to the default `models.CASCADE`
https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/ref/models/fields/#foreignkey


**Related issues**

Django 2.0 support - #85 

Wagtail query about `on_delete` not in docs, but throwing error when not provided to `ParentalKey` - https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/issues/4126

issue_title: OpenCensusServerInterceptor reports incorrect timing
issue_body:
There is a bug in OpenCensusServerInterceptor that causes server spans to be finished immediately. You can reproduce it by running the helloworld grpc example in examples/trace/grpc. You should see a server span with a very short execution time, for example when I run it locally with a print exporter I get:

`{'traceId': 'a8fdeb7813144b178cb4b3d3d9b8fee4', 'spans': [{'childSpanCount': 0, 'displayName': {'truncated_byte_count': 0, 'value': 'grpc_server'}, 'parentSpanId': 4417962580138865, 'startTime': '2018-01-23T16:01:17.826761Z', 'attributes': {'attributeMap': {'/component': {'string_value': {'truncated_byte_count': 0, 'value': 'grpc'}}}}, 'spanId': 7098108131070600, 'endTime': '2018-01-23T16:01:17.826771Z'}]}`

The problem is that [this line](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/ext/grpc/server_interceptor.py#L62) is being traced, but it isn't actually executing the server handler code. It just returns an RPCMethodHandler, which defers execution. 

I hacked up a [quick fix](https://gist.github.com/wkiser/d086a3f623f135265914e2b51bb722e8) that wraps the RPCMethodHandler and instruments the span when the handler is evaluated. A better solution might be to have the RpcMethodHandlerWrapper accept arbitrary callbacks, or to update grpc to have RPCMethodHandler accept callbacks natively.

Happy to submit a PR if you have a preferred approach. Thanks!

issue_title: Check for glue records when needed
issue_body:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7489#section-7.1

i.e. for

```
$ dig +short _dmarc.nhn.no TXT
"v=DMARC1\; p=none\; rua=mailto:dmarc@nhn.no,mailto:dsm1h58m@ag.dmarcian.com\; ruf=mailto:dmarcfull@nhn.no"
```
`ag.dmarcian.com` is external to `nhn.no`, so it needs to specify that it will accept reports for `nhn.no` this way:
 
```bash
$ dig +short nhn.no._report._dmarc.ag.dmarcian.com TXT
"v=DMARC1"
```
